name: Adams, James Walter
motto: Take everybody's advice, then do as you please.
clubs: R. O. T. C. Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Walter left the plains of Alabama to join the Clemson Tigers in the fall of '25. Soon after his arrival they give him the name "Jahnnie" along with numerous others, but the first has outlived all the others. Jabronie cast his lot with the Electricals, but after a year he decided that he would join the Civils, and with them he expects to graduate in June. In this course he has been a success and we expect him to continue so that some day Clemson will justly be the proud of him. To know him to like him, because he is always jolly and a friend to everyone. He is liked not only by the boys, but by the girls as well. Though he will leave his "Tiger" friends in June, he will not be forgotten. We, the Senior Class, wish him the very best of luck in his problems of life.

name: Adams, Ludlow Carlton
motto: Life is a seminar; don't scorn the fellow who is down today, for he may be up tomorrow.
clubs: R. O. T. C. Training Camp, Plattsburg Barracks. N. Y.
Sophomore Junior, and Senior Dancing Club
Clemson College Student Chapter, A. S. C. E.
description: Lundlow, the boy from Meriwether, came to Clemson in the fall of '22 with full intentions of being among the followers of Agriculture, but as the time passed he was convinced that he had made a mistake in the choice of a course. He knew that he was not made for a farmer, so he turned his attention to Civil Engineering as his life's work. LeRoy's aim is to be a Civil Engineer who is looked upon as one who accomplishes something worth while. Go to it, boy, we are all behind you and expect you to make a success in any course you may choose to follow.

name: Alexander, Stephen Ross
motto: Self Reverence, Self Knowledge, Self Control these three alone lead life to Sovereign Power.
clubs: President Electric City Club, Secretary and Treasurer '26
Organizer and Vice President of Bailey Club
Member, Sophomore Junior and Senior Paddling Club
Delegate Student Volunteer Conference, Clinton, S. C.
description: Ross, a product of the Anderson High School and Bailey Military Institute, emerged from the fog in the fall of 1923, and came to the lair of the far-famed Tiger. He felt the want of a higher education; he came after it, and got it. Upon finishing Bailey Military Institute, Ross jumped into the business world and became connected with a well known Anderson cotton firm. After grinding away for more than a year he suddenly realized that his educational motor didn't have the power to propel him up and above the maddening throng. Did he quit? No, he went in search of more intellectual gas; Clemson became his filling station. As for personality, "Alec" has one of those 'winning' ones. He is an optimist, in the strictest sense of the word. He wears a perpetual smile, knows no enemies, but has a host of friends of both sexes. Here's to you "Alec"; we wish you much success.

name: Anderson, Rudolph Duffey
motto: There is no royal road to highest fame, The man has toiled who wears a glorious name.
description: The sorrow that attended the departure of 'R. D.' from Clio for the halls of learning at Clemson in '23 was nothing compared to the sorrow that will be felt when we part from him in the never-to-be-forgotten year of 1927. Although the ranks of the 'Educationalists' were swelled by the addition of 'R. D.' during the four years that he has been with them, he has taken an active interest in anything that would help his Alma Mater. The disciples of 'Sookie' were indeed fortunate to secure such a valuable addition to their number. The loss of him will mean the loss of a man who has always been willing and glad to help others to help themselves. The best that we can wish him is 'R. D.' is nothing more than he well deserves, and we have the highest expectations that he will make as much of a success of life as any one of his college career. Our best wishes go with you, 'R. D.'

name: Harold William Askins
description: Harold, feeling rather ambitious, decided to cast his lot with the Mechanicals, and because of the desire to see something that doesn't exist, he has explored the mysteries of steam with a determination that cannot be excelled. Shortly after reaching Tigertown this young lad realized that four years was a very short time in which to prepare for his chosen profession, and as a result he has been a hard worker. He is a man who thinks a lot and acts only on his convictions. Harold possesses that 'never say die' spirit which is sure to characterize his success in life. You can always find Harold where the fun is thickest, and if he is one your friends you can always rely on him. His congenial disposition, sterling integrity, and wonderful strength of character have won for him the admiration of all who know him. Harold, old boy, may the best in the world be yours.

name: Aull, James Long
motto: If I can lend a strong hand to the fallen, or defend the right against a single envious strain, my life, though bare perhaps of much that seemeth dear and near to us of earth, will not have been in vain.
clubs: Recording Secretary, and President Columbian Literary Society
Member A. S. C. E., '26 and '27
Secretary and Treasurer A. S. C. E. '27
description: “Crip” decided that he could see better things by looking through a telescope, so in the fall of '23 he joined the “Clarke Clan.” Ever since he has been one of the boys. While at Clemson he has made many friends among the faculty and the student. In fact, he has gained the friendship and respect of everyone who has met him. “Crip's” combination of characteristics has helped him wonderfully in gaining so many friends here and at other colleges (where they do not play football). He seems to be at home anywhere, and it seems that he always “fits in.” Besides being one of “King” Cole's Expert Machine Gunners, “Crip” has done many other things which prove that he is fully capable of doing his share of the work. Here's a wish and a bet that “Crip” Atwell will make things hum in this old Solar System.

name: Austin, Paul Barnette
motto: Be your age, Jelly-beans we pass.
clubs: Boxing Team at Camp McClellan, Ala
description: "Plough-boy", For this he is known to everyone, has by friendliness and witty sayings won the admiration of all his classmates. After becoming acquainted with Clemson and the Tiger Spirit, he decided to join the ranks of those who uphold the Purple and Gold on the gridiron. Since making this decision, he has fought like a real Tiger. He chose the Civil Engineering course as his college work, and it is very likely that he will succeed in life as he has here. Class work comes first with him, but it is rumored that he always finds time to write to some fair maiden in Walhalla. Paul, luck to you. May success be yours in whatever you undertake.

name: Avent, James Kirkham
motto: When you get to the bridge, cross it.
clubs: Delegate S. C. College Press Association
Charter Member and First Lt. Sabre Club
R. O. T. C. Camp MacKellan, Ala.
description: Hard working, industrious, amiable, and courteous--that's "Jake." These qualities have bound to him many friends.--Friends not only for college days, but friends for life. To know him is to know a man. The results of his hard work, industriousness, and amiability are best shown in the manner he edited The Tiger and commanded the second battalion. In addition, his class work has not suffered, for he ranks high. "Jake" plans to go into the army after graduation. This will be a loss to the civil engineering profession, but when one wins someone else must lose. As far as records show, Cupid's dart has failed to pierce the heart of this young man. But we do know that if the prize were to fall it would be a rich one for the "ingerior sex". Here is the best of success to you, "Jake." We are proud that we have had the privilege of knowing and working with you. We expect you to be Chief of Staff of the United States Army some day.

name: Baker, John Henry
clubs: Charter Member and Captain Sabre Club
Orangeburg County Club Sect. and Treas. '25-'26
R. O. T. C. Camp MacKellan, Ala.
description: "Still water runs deep," and "Johnny" is true to the maxim. Records showed the worth of a capable, concentrated brain, and opposite these records appeared the name "Baker". With each passing year grew the love and respect for "Johnny" in the hearts of his schoolmates. The prominence and popularity of most men is quickened by fate, reaches a climax, and soon recedes. It shall never be so with "Johnny." Each of the many honors that has been bestowed upon him, and the many prominent offices that he has filled, have been made into sound stepping stones leading upward to the untold heights of success. No words are too colorful, no praising too emphatic to portray accurately this "man among men." Yet, few words may picture him, were the men of the world lined up and a selection made of a friendship that the best would cherish. "Johnny" would stand foremost.

name: Baker, Whiteford Lee
motto: Excelsior!
clubs: Secretary. Treasurer. President, Chesterfield County Club
Winner of Medal Best Poem in Chronicle '26
Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C. Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: In the fall of '23 there emerged from the sand dunes of Chesterfield a lad, bound for new worlds to conquer. He chose Clemson as his objective and therefore entered the ranks of 'radom' with a crash. During the four years he has been here he has done justice in his work—displaying outstanding abilities, sharing equal honors with the best in his class. His name is inscribed among the few who have met the requirements and qualifications of distinction. His outside activities have been mostly literary. The Chronicle has been replete with his many poems. 'W. L.' is a true friend and gentleman—a man whose friendship is golden. So, Here's to you, old boy! May many fond memories of old C. A. C. linger with you and help you to 'carry on' and never forget the 'Class of '27 and your Alma Mater.

name: Baldwin, Harold Lawrence
motto: Live and let live.
clubs: R. O. T. C. Camp. Plattsburg, N. Y.
description: Harold is one of the boys who have come to us from the "town on the Edisto." As we review the history of the Class of '27 we see him, a smiling, cheerful, happy young lad, and his four years with us have made of a loyal Tiger an even more loyal one. Harold has chosen as his work Electrical Engineering, since he must have realized that Engineering is the art of taking the material at hand and producing a finished product that is polished and ready for service. From "Crip" and the others, he has absorbed some of the methods of that process. He is one of the men who have given little thought to distance and transportation when it came to taking a trip. He has always succeeded in arriving at his destination without any trouble. However, when we come to the end of a class history, we find a man who has always been a true Tiger, an honest man standing four-square for the right, a little man with a big heart and a winning personality. He is a true friend, and a gentleman, and to know him is to love him. May success be yours, Harold; we know it awaits you.

name: Beam, Fred Albert
motto: Love many, trust few, but always paddle your own canoe.
clubs: Senior R. O. T. C. Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: A rare combination, one that is seldom found, this--always a pleasing countenance, and a store of ready wit and talent. Fred is the embodiment of these striking traits. None know him but to like him; his sterling character and jovial good nature have won him many friends and a warm place in the hearts of every classmate. Fred has that touch of "stickability" that always results in the accomplishment of what he sets out to do. In many forms of college activities we find Fred a steady booster; he has been a loyal "Tiger," and judging from his correspondence we would think that he is loyal to one of the fairer sex. Fred has chosen Agronomy as his major course. Go to it, Fred. We are wishing you a happy future along with the choice of your heart.

name: Bennett, Charles Clifton
motto: An acre of performance is worth a whole world of promise.
description: Friends, here you have a true fighter, one who holds his ground to the last ditch. As one of Uncle Sam's fighting 'Donghboys' in the 'old Hickory' division, he helped to break the famous Hindenburg line. We envy him the wound he received in his country's service. Here we have proof of the fighting spirit of this Tiger. Charles joined the Tigers fresh from his ventures into the field of matrimony. (This occasion in his life probably spurred him on.) He has pursued his studies in his chosen field of Horticluture faithfully and well. His conscientious and diligent work has won for him the unreserved respect of his professors. He numbers many friends among his classmates because of his frankness and pleasing disposition. Keep lighting, Charles. We predict for you a bright future.

name: Bennett, William Marshall
motto: There is no pathway of flowers leading to glory.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: The little town of Ashton gave up a worthy son when Marshall decided to enter Clemson in the fall of '23. Since that time he has gained many friends and proved himself an earnest worker. We do not know just what this aggressive youngster's plans are for the future, but we believe he intends to specialize in some branch of science. There is where we wish him success. Class work comes first with him, but he is a well known 'Slick', he can always find time to write to that fair maiden at Withrop College. M. B., your natural ability, combined with the inspiration of that little girl, is sure to make a success of you. Success for you is our fondest wish.

name: Berry, William Jackson
motto: Why worry, it will happen.
clubs: R. O. T. C. Camp. Plattsburg, N. Y.
description: In the fall of 1923 Clemson gathered to her fold a nobleman of nature one 'Bill' Berry by name. To know him is to discover a heart of purest gold encased within a cloak of honest friendliness and sincerity. Although he has not yet started the world with any wondrous works, nor has he thrilled a grandstand with the feats of an athletic Nimrod, he has accomplished a feat more enviable than either of these. He has acquired the esteem and respect of all who know him, things that gold cannot buy. The crystal sphere prophesies a fruitful future for him.

name: Bonnette, George Washington
motto: Love all; trust few; do wrong to none.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McDougall, Ala.
description: This young man joined the ranks of the Tiger clan in the fall of '23. We all agree that Ridge Springs lost one of her most worthy lads, but Clemson profited by the loss; for George is a loyal Tiger, a true friend, and a gentleman. He is a man of high ideals and possesses a strong character. This lad has a smile for everyone, and is always ready to lend a helping hand to the unfortunate. He has won a warm place in the hearts of the students and all who have known him. From the interest he has taken recently in Winthrop College, we predict that he will soon take one of the fairer sex to share his happiness. Best wishes and good luck to you, George, old boy. The Class of '27 feels confident that success is yours.

name: Brabham, Henry Kearse
clubs: Charter member and Second Lieutenant Sales Club
Secretary and Treasurer Bamberg County Club, '26-'26
R. O. T. C. Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: With several years of Prep School preparation, 'Doe' entered Clemson with a determination which, backed by ability, has molded for this institution and this state a wonderful prospect of future years. His works and achievements here have been great in every respect. Seriousness is an asset possessed by few college men. 'Doe' possesses this envied attribute. Reserve, true friendship, deep sense of duty, and loyalty to every act and friend also go to make a combination seldom found among boys. Duty before pleasure, friends before self, and thought before speech or action—all these can be justly applied to Brabham. Although quiet and unassuming, 'Doe' has been honored with a great many leading positions in college and student activities. His scholastic records show steady work in his chosen course. He leaves us with his record unblemished. May the best in yours, 'Doe'. May you success be great and your troubles few. We shall expect great things of you in life.

name: Albert Dargan Breland
motto: A Gentleman never lets studying interfere with his education.
clubs: Expert Machine Gunner at R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
Member of '26 Live Stock Judging Team
description: Hailing from the mossy and sunny land of the Edisto River section his life is necessarily a romantic one. His is the history which should be written in Meter,——a life made up of witty jokes and merry repartee. 'Buddie' claims that he was once a ladies' man but we believe he was, and that he quit from an over dose of female attention. His 'line' betrays him, though, and we have a lingering suspicion that he was a salesman for a matrimonial bureau, since he is so adept at getting his friends in "Dutch", yet manages to stay free and single himself. He can tell you what kind of a girl she is by her stationery, and the workings of the ladies' minds are as clear to him as the Evolution problem. Coming from 'Greevich land' he possesses a rare brogue which he, alone, can understand, if slightly excels. However, 'Buddie' is an earnest worker and a thorough student, a true friend to all, well-deserving the many friends that he has made during his stay at Clemson.

name: Brock, John Leland
motto: The only way to have a friend is to be one.
clubs: Member Clemson R. O. T. C. Track Team, Camp McClellan, Ala.
Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C. Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: In the fall of '23 there came to Clemson a lad who has won for himself many friends through his splendid personality and good nature. Leland decided from the first that he wanted to become an Electrical Engineer, so he cast his lot with 'Crip' and he is now, according to 'Crip', a rival of the Class of '06. Leland is a product of Central High School and this institution may well be proud of him. Besides holding his own in classes, he has made quite a name for himself in athletics. No truer Tiger than Leland has ever walked the Clemson campus and we wish him all the luck he deserves in the future.

name: Brodie, George Harold
motto: Remember the steam kettle though up to its neck in hot water it continues to sing.
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C. Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Sit up and take notice, dear readers. The above picture represents one of Salley's proud citizens. Sally is very proud of him, and proud she should be, for she is a very fine fellow. Brodie, as we know about him, (but 'Harold' to Columbia College)entered Clemson in the fall of '23. He decided at once to become one of 'Crip's' proteges, and cast his lot with the electrical engineers. Quite wise was his decision, for he is a very adaptable fellow, and makes good at anything he undertakes. We may expect Salley to be revolutionizing the electrical industry in the near future. Brodie is a boy whom everybody likes. He makes friends everywhere. He has only one weakness and that is 'girls'. But it also seems that the girls have a weakness when they encounter this fair lad from Aiken County. Here's luck to you, Harold, and may your luck be evenly distributed between Perry and Sally.

name: Brown, William Carroll
clubs: Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Club
Sophomore, Junior and Senior 'Hop' Reception Committees
description: To the world at large this lad is known as W. Carroll Brown, but such a title fails to express the wholesome manliness that the name 'Buck' rightly infers. Intellectually, 'Buck' far exceeds the average student, which fact his college record attests in vivid certainty. But his intellectuality is not of the narrow, momory-efficient type. He has discernment, forethought, keen analytical power, and enviable creative and imaginative capacities. All his judgments are of a cool, unerring type, yet there is a geniality and warmth about his criticisms that provokes admiration. He has a bigness of mind and personality that escape exact definition. Weightly, impressive, striking, he is never so mechanically polished that he loses the enchantment of personal warmth and enthusiasm. His life is the equilibrium of mental excellence, strength of will, and emotional moderation. His career has been a following of the paths of the 'Golden Mean'.

name: Bryce, George Wilson
motto: Be a friend to all, but love only one.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Wilson, the fair blonde lad from Florence, has made his impression on the hearts of his fellow classmates during his four years in our midst by being always rarin' for a bit' excitement. He has used enough tires and gasoline going to Rock Hill to make Henry Ford's Fords, and John D. Rockefeller's gas business and assured success. If 'Winthero' loves us like he loves Winthrop, we look for nuptials as soon as they both find it out. He knows, personally, eleven hunderd cadets and has eleven hundred friend at Clemson. He will always be a success because he always strives to succeed wherever he goes. Whatever his task in life is, he will be surrounded by well-wishing friends. Here's luck to you, Wilson.

name: Burnett, William Edwin
motto: Hope springs eternal in the human breast; Man never is, but always to be blest.
clubs: R. O. T. C. Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Hambone", as he is familiarly known by the large majority of the cadet corps, came to Clemson in the fall of '23 from the metropolis of Wellford. Even as a "rat" he gained a reputation for his fun and good nature, a reputation which he has lived up to ever since. This, together with his perseverance and his willingness to help, has made friends of everyone who knows him. When he came to the parting of the ways between Electrical and Mechanical Engineering at the beginning of the senior year, "Hambone" chose the Mechanical way. His choice was quite a fortunate step for those with whom he was to be associated, for everybody who has come in contact with him, has gone away benefited in some way. The many good qualities which he has shown cannot fail to carry him to the top and to give to him the success which he so justly deserves. Our best wishes go with you, "Hambone", as you go from our midst.

name: Calloun, William Bryant
motto: Don't go under the ladder - climb it.
clubs: Sophomore Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
R. O. T. C. Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: A ladies' man, but nobody's fool. Yeah, that's "Brunt". Broke more hearts in South Carolina than there are dogs in Georgia, but he's no "cake-eater". To know Callou is to know a regular fellow. He is never too busy to spare a word of encouragement to a down-hearted friend, or a word of praise to a friend in exaltation.

name: Cannon, James Patterson
motto: May we never lack friends, who, like shadows, keep close to us in the sunshine, only to desert us on a cloudy day, or in the night.
clubs: Two mile run, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan '26
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Lo! Here is a man who can think and act for himself. This lad hails from Honea Path; and Honea Path's loss was Clemson's gain. In 'Jimmie' is embodied those traits that make a real man. He has not only demonstrated himself in the classroom, but also on the Cross-country course, and in track as well. 'Jimmie' has made scores of friends at Clemson, and fortunate indeed is the man who can call him friend for here he has one who will stick by him through thick and thin and never reckon the consequences. With his ability, his high ideals, his power of determination, we cannot predict anything but a bright future for him in the Mechanical world. Luck to you, 'Jimmie'.

name: Carter, Troy Hudson
motto: He who fears ridicule is at the mercy of every fool.
description: Carter, commonly known to us as Troy, came to Clemson in the fall of '23, determined to waste his time taking Agriculture, but as all great men do, Troy realized his first big mistake in life, therefore he changed courses and decided to take the best course at Clemson College, Textile Engineering. It took a good man to make good the work that Troy did in order to finish with the Class of '27, but of course he was equal to the task. During the time that Troy spent at Tigertown, his friends looked upon him as being a boy of fine character, high morals, and determination. With these qualities blended together one cannot look for anything but success of the highest degree; and we are sure that before long, he will be one of the leading 'bobbin' dodgers' of the South.

name: Cash, Daniel Hydrick
motto: The world looks brighter from behind a smile.
clubs: R. O. T. C. Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Dan", as he is known to his many friends at Clemson, hails from the "city" of Chesnee. He entered Clemson in the fall of '23, and has held his own with his class throughout his entire stay here. After careful consideration, he decided to cast his lot with those students who expect to improve the dairy industry in South Carolina. With his determination, will power, and earnest efforts, we predict nothing but success for him in his chosen field of work. Long to you, "Dan." the Class of '27 is expecting great things of you in the future.

name: Caughman, Ernest Miles
motto: If at first you don't succeed; try, try, again.
clubs: R. O. T. C. Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "E. M." came to the Tiger's Lair in the fall of '23. The people of Lexington were proud to know that one from their midst was leavint them to seek higher knowledge. He has learned to be a true "Tiger" during his stay with us. "E. M." cast his lot with the "Bulls." It is well that he chose that line, for he is well schooled in the art of "Shooting Bull." He has many friends--all won by his friendliness, pins a willingness to help others. He claims to be a woman hater, but his "line" betrays him. Probably he is an agent for cosmetic preperations and wants to keep it a secret. To say that he is a prince of a fellow would be putting it mildly."E. M.", we all wish you much luck in the Animal World.

name: Causey, Otis Rembrant
motto: Not on the heights, but climbing.
clubs: Sharpshooter, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: When "O. R." boarded the train for Clemson in the fall of '23 there was heard above the roar of sirens and fog horns a mournful drone, "What is it?" Yes, quite right! The wails of Weeping Wisdom. She knew her doom was sealed for Causey was off in war paint and on her trail. Others had found her an elusive will o' the wisp. At last a determined little warrior with a spirit of never-say-die was abroad, and when such is the case, victory needs must crown him. Yes, that Causey straight through. A plucky little fellow, a friend true as steel, and a scholar of estimable worth. He is a Tarheel by adoption but a Sandhapper at heart with innumerable friends among the fairer sex. His many "affaires de cour" are living testimonials to the fact that romance is not dead. Here's to you, Causey. We have known you and found you a man. What more needs be said? Luck to you.

name: Chapman, Hoyt
motto: Make life happy today, it may be miserable tomorrow.
description: Here we have the object of all torpedoes, especially from the Freshmen. Chapman's lack of dignity, with cheerful disposition, made him such. But his high ideals and firm convictions have molded all these "Sinkings" into everlasting friendships. "Gerald," as he is known to the mountain lassies, cast his lot with the railway world and came to Clemson to better fit himself for his chosen profession. Believing that variety is the spice of life, he took every course from Military Science to Bible and was ever present with that very familiar little "tee hee" which so often shocked Tubby Braden. Hoyt is too precious to get along well with the girls, but every time his heart is broken he throws it more diligently into his work and forgets the breaker. Therefore, we predict for him great success in love in the railway world--but we don't ever expect to see him drilling freight trains in military formation.

name: Chapman, William Fred
motto: Be a friend to all, but love only one.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: The Class of '27 could never have been complete without this jolly, good fellow. Fred hails from Belton, and he is a firm believer in the old town. He chose the way of the "Lord" (Collings) as the path he would travel through College. All of his spare time he has devoted to business transactions in the candy and confectionery line, and today stands as the greatest financial "wizard" of the class. We predict great things for him in any line of work he may undertake. Fred with his ever present smile and characteristic cackle laugh, is a true friend to all.

name: Chreitzberg, Charles Herbert
motto: Give to the world the best that you have, and the best will come back to you.
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Alabama
description: Charlie came to us in the fall of '23 from the city of Williamston. He came to Clemson for several reasons—the most important was to get the preliminary training necessary before entering the Textile world. He is a diligent worker, and is striving for the things that are worth while. His one weakness is the power that the fairer sex has over him. His pleasing and unassuming personality has won him a warm place in the hearts of his many friends. He is a true friend, a loyal Tiger, and is always ready to help anyone in any way he can. It is not necessary to predict a future for this lad because we all know he will be a real success in all walks of life. So here's to you, Charlie. We wish you the best of luck and happiness in the future.

name: Cooper, John Robert Royal
motto: "That man must daily wiser grow, whose search is bent himself to know."
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: In the fall of '23 Cooper came from the "City by the water tank" to enter into the study of the electron with the rest of the Electricals under the supervision of "Crip." When we think of Cooper in the days that come after graduation, we will visualize a loyal Tiger, a true sport, and a gentleman. Besides his accomplishments in college activities, he has made an excellent record in the classroom. During the past four years he has won the admiration of his classmates. He is a hard worker. He is not afraid to undertake a hard task and fight it to the end. He has always taken an active part in the work of the Y. M. C. A., and has always been ready to help Clemson in any way he could. His cheerful disposition, willing hand, and pleasing personality have won for him many friends. With the determination and ability that are yours, we can wish nothing for you but success.

name: Corbett, Thomas Burnice
motto: "Speak low, if you speak love."
clubs: Student Branch American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: None knew him but to like him, none named him but to praise. We are proud to call him our classmate. It was by his attractive personality and cheerful disposition that "Ig," as he is best known at Clemson, won the friendship and admiration of all of his associates during the past four years. Who would have thought that Walterboro was willing to part with this wonderful little lad for four long years. Thanks to Walterboro for lending us "Ig." He is the type of fellow that will stick by a friend. This handsome young lad craved no military honors, but took an active interest in his class work. We all wonder just why "Ig" did not take Electricity. We know by good authority that he is a great lover of the fairer sex, and if we can judge from the frequent letters he receives, he will soon be in a little bungalow just large enough for two. Here's to "Ig," we wish you success in business, happiness in marriage; and may your troubles be few.

name: Cox, Floyd Malcolm
motto: Let me live in the house by the side of the road and be a friend to man.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Out of the shadow of the standpipe in the City of Belton crept a shy and lowly candidate for "rathood." Ere he had reached Tigertown, he was permanently dubbed "Ruben," and soon won for himself a place in the hearts of his fellowmen. Jovial, candid, sincere, and possessed with magnetism of personality-these are the characteristics which have carried "Ruben" along so pleasingly during his years at Clemson. And these same qualities will bear him forward on the rugged road to success, adding stamina and progress to the community that will be graced by his homestead.

name: Crain, Monroe Crawford
motto: Remember, the man who growls at everything leads a dog's life.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Monroe entered Clemson in the fall of '22. After two years he stopped for a session, but he came back to us with the determination of completing the work he had started. He cast his lot with the "Ags" and finally came under the care of "Lord" Collings. Monroe is a gentleman, a friend, and a true Tiger. He takes his troubles by the smooth side, and never seems to let anything worry him. During his stay among us he has won many friends because of his pleasing personality, and helping hand. Although full of fun, he takes his work seriously enough to choose wisely what is best. Here's to you, Monroe. As you journey along life's pathway, may you meet with nothing but success; may your joys be larger than the ocean and your sorrows lighter than its foam.

name: Cromer, Lewis Ezra
motto: Do your best, and if it's a mistake, hide nothing and be glad to suffer for it.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: A sudden, despondent group of fellows have gathered. The world seems to have no charm, and life is bleak and dreary. The door opens. A glow of enthusiasm, warmth, and good fellowship immediately fills the room. Frowns give place to smiles and the new comer establishes himself a little dearer in the hearts of his friends. Thus may we picture "Jake". In disposition he is a paragon. His very being is a smile, but not the artificial type that bores. He possesses a keen intellect and an originality of wit that one seldom is graced with. The ability to judge human nature is termed "A Gift from Heaven". If this be true, Heaven never opened its flood gate more bountifully on one mortal than on Ezra. And along with his gracious endowment came a quality worshiped by the gods - sincerity. "Jake", during his four years at college, has woven many, many unspeakable friendships that will follow him through life.

name: Cromer, Nevitt Crosby
motto: Shining as a beacon light to all endeavor of a human soul is the humble reward, Well Done.
clubs: Palmetto Literary Society, two years, third term president
Machine Gun Company, Camp McClellan, R. O. T. C., Sharpshooter
description: In September, 1923, "Nev" left the Electric City to enlarge his knowledge. He decided at an early age to cast his lot with the Clemson Tigers. In fact he hardly waited until he was old enough for a "rat", but was generally classed as a "mouse" his Freshman year. In spite of the fact that he was only a wee "rat", he began at the outset with a determination to make good. This determination has lasted through the four years of his college life. "Nev" has made many friends on account of his quiet and pleasing disposition. His habits are clean and he has a determination to stick to them. Judging from his excellent work and his type of character, we can predict nothing but success for him.

name: Cureton, Robert Hamilton
motto: Lend thine ears to many, thy tongue to few
description: He is a rather quiet lad, but this does not keep him from being known. He is liked by everyone. His striking personality and sterling character combine to make of him a typical figure of manhood. He has always stood high in his work and strived for more than a mere "get by". He is one who lets everything come as it may and meets all obstacles with a firm determination to succeed. After having the misfortune to lose a year at Clemson, he changed his course from Mechanical to Civil. The time lost was not wasted, however, as it was used in fitting him for the course for which he is best suited. He has become one of the most ardent followers of "Will Rogers", and we all predict for him a successful future as a civil engineer.

name: Cullum, Frederick Eldridge
motto: Keep Digging.
description: In the fall of '23 the Twin City, Batesburg-Leesville, was thrown into the chaos of distress, because of the unexpected exodus of one of its most noted celebrities. Where, why, and how he had gone was a puzzling mystery to his many friends in the quiet city that was proud to claim him as her own. He had decided to become a Tiger. Furthermore, he chose Civil Engineering as his life's work; his chief ambition being to dam up Cripple Creek, a booming brook whose gushing, irresistible torrent had often wafted his sailboats into the land of the unknown. He has not let his studies interfere with his health, but nevertheless he has developed into a proficient engineer. The flying flame has made a name for himself on the cinder paths of the South; he being the holder and sole owner of South Carolina's State record for the one mile run. His fine sportsmanship and sterling personality have made friends of all who know him. We're for you. May your great dam forever repulse the waters of Cripple Creek and may you overcome any obstacle that may blacken your paths to success.

name: Dill, Robert Carl
motto: The reward for honesty is in the act itself.
clubs: M. G. Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
Vice Chairman, A. I. E. E., 1926
description: He made his appearance on the campus in January of '24, and from then on he cast his lot with "Crip's" Electrical Wizards of Clemson College. Carl has always been a life saver in the classroom in that he can answer a question at the psychological moment when the rest of the class is "up in the air" and wondering what the outcome of the period will be. Carl is a true Tiger in every sense of the word. The best wishes of the Class of '27 are with you Carl; may you attain the goal for which you have been so faithfully striving.

name: Dobson, Cecil Rhodes
motto: Determination is the key-note to success.
description: Cecil came to Tigertown in the fall of '23 from the thriving metropolis of Lancaster, and he departs from us now to return home, as an educated man in every respect and especially along the line of Architecture. We always find Cecil greeting us with a pleasant smile. He is an earnest, hard worker and his troubles are always the least of his worries. The lights are never dimmed in his room before midnight and sometimes later. We have always found Cecil to be an earnest promoter of this much favored quotation, "If thou be rich, do not idle away thy time in luxury." Cecil, the Class of '27 wishes you the best of luck and it will always cherish your memory.

name: Donaldson, John Henry
motto: 'Tis the mind that makes the body rich.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Donaldson journeyed up to Tigertown in the fall of '23 to show the followers of Grosner Goodhue a few things about the complex subject of Architecture. This idea was soon dismissed and now, after four years of diligent work, he is ready to show the world what training plus natural ability can accomplish. Donaldson's principal talent is in the field of art, but he has all the other qualities which go to make a good student as is shown by his class standing and a host of friends. He has been one of the most loyal and orderly cadets of his class for four years. A gentleman.

name: Dorn, Thomas Elbert, Jr.
motto: Stand for what you think is right, though all the world be against you.
clubs: Corresponding Secretary, President, Calhoun Literary Society
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This lad hails from the city of Greenwood. While Clemson has heartily welcomed the gain in capturing this man, his home town has grieved over a great loss. "Willie," as he is known, came to us with the determination to delve into the many mysteries of scientific Agriculture. In his four years' stay here he has been very active in his literary society and has shown unusual talent as a speaker. He has worked hard for "Sookie" and has received the reward which we all covet. "Willie" has made a host of friends since his arrival at Tigertown. He is a loyal Tiger, a good soldier, and a true friend. Because of his big heart and cheerful personality he will long be remembered by his classmates. Here's to you, "Willie," luck, success, and happiness.

name: Dowling, Charles B. Jr.
motto: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
clubs: R. O. T. C. Camp, Plattsburg, N. Y.
description: "C. B." is an all-round good fellow. He is a true friend, an earnest worker, and a good athlete, although he hasn't won a block of block letters. The electrical world cannot help waiting impatiently for him to join it in June; for he will be a great asset to any organization. After a few years' labor with dynamos, motors, entropy diagrams, etc., he is ready to step into the shoes left by the illustrious Steinmetz. What Edison, Steinmetz, and Marconi have not done, "C. B." will do.

name: Duggan, Olaf Roughton
motto: Try to make yourself an honest man and then you may be sure that there is one less rascal in the world.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer A. S. M. E. '27
Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Dugan" came to Clemson in the fall of 1923, and after looking over the College and Faculty decided that Clemson could give him the knowledge he so greatly desired. He cast his lot with the Electrics and was determined to learn whether Steinmetz and "Crip" knew as much about ohms and watts as he had heard they did. Four years of steady work have convinced him that they know quite a bit about these subjects, but he says that there are still a few things that he doesn't see how they get. Not only has this lad made good in class work but with his sunny disposition, his willingness to serve, and his loyalty he has won for himself a host of friends during his four years at Clemson. With your determination and ability, the Class of '27 can see nothing but success for you. Best of luck, Roughton!

name: Etheredge, Thomas Jackson, Jr.
motto: It is better to love that which you cannot have, than to have that which you cannot love.
clubs: Sharpshooter, Howitzer Platoon, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: STOP!! LOOK!! LISTEN!! Here he is. Another fine specimen from Batesburg-Leesville. They should be proud of him. Tom came to Clemson in the fall of '23, at which time he selected Electrical Engineering as his course. Etheredge is the owner of a fascinating personality which has netted him many friends. He is also a hard worker, always willing to do his part of anything. Tom deserves much credit for he received his B. S. degree under "Red" and "Crip" and few there are that can stand this line. Tom has been a great aid to the government during his stay at Clemson, in that he has helped finance the Postal Service. Not only the two-cent stamps that he has used every day himself, but also the ones he induced someone else to use have helped to accomplish this. "Toby," as he is known to one of the fairer sex, is everything that the word "man" implies; he is every inch a gentleman, and nothing but the best can be expected of him.

name: Faust, Curtis Cornelius
motto: The highway to progress is the path of truth.
clubs: Member of Calhoun Literary Society, Columbian Literary Society, '26-'27
Jail Bird Club, Clean Sleeve Club
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Whenever there was anything to be said or done, C. C. Faust was always there. He has shown a great interest in the artistic phases of everything and has devoted much time to the uplifting of the better and higher ideals of college life. If we follow his suggestions, movements of a distinctly progressive nature will undoubtedly be unleashed—all for the promotion of a grander Alma Mater. May your dreams crystallize into realities, "Doc". We all wish you happiness and success.

name: Fishburne, Frances Joseph
motto: Friendship is love without his wings.
clubs: Assistant Big Chief Red Head Club
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Porter Military Academy laid his excellent foundation for college work; Charleston sharpened his tongue to its traditionary brogue; his hair got wet and forthwith rusted—and thus "Fish" escaped and sought refuge at Clemson. As a stranger "Fish" is interesting, but as an acquaintance he becomes "Pinkhead" and likable accordingly. His almost subtle art of speedily acquiring lasting friendship is worthy of any man's emulation. As deftly as the Thief of Bagdad he steals his way into a prominent chamber of your heart, and with his easy but elaborate smiles defies dislodgement. Thus, well it is that Electricity dominates his ambitions in life, for with his continual perseverance, success must ultimately be his. May you gratefully enjoy it, "Fish".

name: Franklow, Marconi Lester
motto: Nothing is either good or bad, but thinking makes it so.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Macaroni", as we know him, came to "Tigertown" from the thriving and well known city of Leesville. He felt that he should get out of his shell and see the world; Clemson was the first stop he made, but in the future he hopes to go on farther where we do not know, but we hope he does. "Macaroni" has made a number of friends at Clemson and if nothing happens he will succeed in getting him a wife. However, we wish you luck and may success be yours.

name: Garrison, Robert Henry
motto: There isn't time in life to make two reputations—so watch your first one.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Anderson County Club, '26
Member Clemson R. O. T. C. Championship Baseball Team, Camp McClellan, Ala.
Senior R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This loyal "Tiger" hails from the nearby village of Sandy Springs, which, though but a speck on the map, has furnished Clemson with some of her best men. "Bob" has a pleasing personality that wins the esteem of those around him and gains him friends on every hand. This, with his natural ability, has accounted in large measure for his rapid climb to a captaincy in the King's army. "Bob" is courteous to a nicety which leads to that finer and fuller trait of character and comradeship. We can foresee nothing but success for one so industrious and so determined as "Bob". Therefore, we look with expectancy to a rapid rise to the heights of his profession in the realm of education. But alas! "Bob" has succumbed to the charms of one of Anderson's fairest. If letters, coming in such numbers, have any bearing on the greatness of love, it is ours to predict that they will share their happiness together soon after graduation.

name: Gaston, Lamar Dewitt
motto: Friends, if we be honest with ourselves, we shall be honest with each other.
description: Gaston joined the Clemson Tigers in his Sophomore year. He entered Davidson College in the fall of '23 and took his Freshman work there. During his stay with us he has always proved himself a gentleman, a true and loyal "Tiger". We have found in him qualities of true manhood, honesty, sincerity, and diligence. He is a lover of his books and a hard worker, and always stands for the right. Such qualities are sure to prove themselves a master key to the door of his future success. His congenial personality has won a host of friends who are wishing him the best of luck as he goes forth into the Electrical World.

name: Gerrard, Frank Harrison
motto: As necessity is the mother of invention, strong desire is the mother of attainment.
description: "Jerry" entered the portals of learning at Clemson with the determination to make a reputation of his own. Through the long years of hard study his ambition has become intensified all the more. He has had a vision, a goal in view. He has a natural love for nature and plants. Plants have an appeal for him as for no other. Nature speaks to him in intimacy. Few plants there are that this ardent and zealous lover of nature is not able to chase down and name. His determination and perseverance have overcome untold obstacles that lay in the path of his search for knowledge. From all outward signs, Gerard is due to place Burbank's accomplishments "in the shade". Jerry's kindly disposition, his genial and witty mimicry have won him a place secure in the hearts of his associates. His amiability insures his keeping many friends.

name: Gibson, Dewey Andrell
motto: He who lives but for himself lives but for a little thing.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Where there's smoke there's fire", and if you trace the invisible smoke which is ever present when "Mid-nite" is near, you will find at the end of your search an intense flame which burns with eternal brightness. This fearless youth is the one and only Dewey Gibson, and never has there been his like. Unique and original, he is characterized by his wild unrest—he is always wanting to do something exciting, and if things seem dull he can always evolve some scheme of amusement. To some, "Mid-nite" is a deep study, but to those who know him he is read as readily as the pages of a first-grade reader. His gaiety, merry jest, and sprightly wit keep him in the lime light of his many friends. By his inherent grace and ease of manner he lives at the pinnacle of the "beau monde". He loves the bright lights of society, but his sense of judgment keeps him from being blinded. "Flow, wine; smile, woman; and 'Mid-nite' is consoled". He is surrounded with friends at Clemson and elsewhere, and because his friends have sought a friend who is "Now and Forever", they have chosen "Mid-nite".

name: Gillespie, Sidney Lockwood
motto: There is a tide in the affairs of men which taken at flood leads on to fortune.
clubs: Member of A. I. E. E., '26, '27
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Lockwood, better known to his many friends at Clemson as "Puss," embarked on this sea of good fortune in the fall of 1923 when he chose Clemson as his Alma Mater. When the new faces began to appear about the campus, this youngster from "The Town of Loving Hearts" was among the first to begin work as an Engineering Student. In the months and years that followed he has won his way into our hearts and has so applied himself that he stands out as one of the best students that Clemson has produced. Four years have passed, and as a disciple of both "Crip" and "Sam," "Puss" has proved that he is made of the stuff that counts. As the years pass on, may the best in the world be for you, a true Tiger, and a friend without equal.

name: Goff, Homer Buford
motto: It is the mind that makes the man, and our vigor is in our immortal soul.
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Friends, this is "Bully". From Leesville, this lad came all the way to be a Tiger. Immediately upon entering, he donned the football uniform and went out to make the Freshman squad. This he did with ease. In class, he is never at a loss for an answer to the "prof's" question, and thus, he has made a record of which to be proud. Characteristic of Homer is his sunny disposition. With it and his ability to charm all comers, he has made many friends while here. He is sound of brain, intelligent, and blessed with native ability. With such traits and such conservativeness as he possesses, he will succeed wherever he goes. "Bully", may Fortune smile your way. You deserve nothing less than such.

name: Goodyear, Cecil Pound
motto: It is better to be a 'has been' than a 'might have been' by far; It is not what you have been, but what you are.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: At the opening of school in the fall of 1923 the campus was again covered with many bright faces. Among these was a fair young lad from Nichols. In the four years that are now history the Class of '27 has learned to love and admire him. He was none other than the ever smiling Cecil Goodyear. During his stay at Clemson, Cecil has taken a great interest in the activities of the college. He has been serving on the Friendship Council of the Y. M. C. A. for three years, and in his senior year, was elected a member of the "Y" Cabinet and President of the Friendship Council. Cecil's fine personality has won for him many friends among the fairer sex as well as on the campus, and no doubt in a short while he will have some girl to share the great future that life has in store for him. Luck to you, Cecil, we are expecting great things of you.

name: Googe, William Jeremiah
motto: Believe everything that everyone tells you, And then do as you please.
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Fatty", as we all know him, entered Clemson in the fall of '23. He first took it upon himself to plan and then design a great cathedral, therefore, he started out to take Architecture. At the beginning of his Sophomore year he decided that he had rather become a "Steinmetz". He then became a very loyal and staunch follower of "Crip" and "Red". Quite well has he succeeded for he is very adapted to "Crip's" course. We may expect great things of him in the electrical world. Googe is a big robust sort of chap, always brimming over with laughter. He is a boy that everybody likes. His magnetic personality gains for him friends everywhere, and his determination always brings him success. If anyone is in need of help of any kind he only has to go to "Fatty". Many are the times we have been in his room and heard people ask him for help of different kinds. Always we have heard him cheerfully say, "I will be glad to if I can." A man of Googe's personality and adaptability is sure to succeed, no matter what he undertakes.

name: Grice, Heyward Simkins
motto: ‘Tis impotent to grieve for what is past, And unavailing to exclaim.
description: Well, here is a Tiger who originated in or near the township of Ward. What this little city lost in his leaving was counteracted by the gain Clemson experienced in his coming. Heyward, a noble, friendly lad, possesses all the traits necessary for a gentleman. Being ever for the right regardless of the cost, is characteristic of him. While he doesn't pose as one of the ten distinguished by virtue of class record, he is easily capable of excellent work. And but for a bit of "lead," lodged in his anatomy, there would be nothing to check his progressiveness. "Rip" has initiative and other such requisites essential to success. Here, Ladies, is your chance. Remember only, that from what we know, it will take effort to conquer this highly esteemed youth. Heyward, the Class can see a successful future for you. Good luck.

name: Greene, James Wightman
motto: The true worth of a man is his character.
clubs: Greenville City Club, Secretary and Treasurer, '27
Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala
description: This lad came to Clemson in the fall of '23 to delve into the mysteries of electricity. "Jimmie" has worked and has done well; he has tried and has succeeded. On the athletic field and in the ring he has displayed his wonderful agility and quickness, and in so doing, has placed Clemson far ahead of self. His name is a synonym for "grit." If "Blocks" were awarded for this kind of spirit "Jimmie" would have had his long since. Take him, men, as a model of the old "Tiger Spirit". When Clemson boasts, she mentions little "Jimmie" Greene. Friendships have not been neglected by him. "Jimmie" may count every Clemson man his friend. All of us know and remember his ready, genial smile and his hearty welcome. A more sincere and loyal friend is hard to find. As "Jimmie" goes out into this old world he takes with him the best wishes of the Tigers, and it seems that we can hear the old Clemson Tiger growling deep in his throat, mourning the loss of his favorite son. May the best of the world be his.

name: Hamrick, Fred Garland
motto: Ye shall know the truth and the truth shall make ye free.
clubs: National Association of Political and Social Science
description: In the autumn of '23 a Tiger roamed into this lair. He was not a "cub" in years at that time. The staid old hills of Cherokee must have bowed even lower when Hamrick strolled away again for they had but recently got him back from war's grim arms, whence he had gone with the Bugle's call in '17. The war left him physically exhausted, but with a keen and receptive mind and with an ardor and enthusiasm that has never dimmed. When we came here we found him older in years and wisdom, but a wonderful "pal" just the same. He has never been obtrusive, but always honest and reserved, and true-blue to the core. Possessed of such a noble philosophy of life as he is, his goal can but be success, our sublimest faith and sincerest hopes are with him. So here's to you, the staunchest pal a fellow could have. We doff our hats to you--a man, and know the world will find in you the same.

name: Hardee, John Harstean
motto: Honest, hard work, and fair play; these shall be the rules and guide of my life in all its phases.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This quiet lad, who hails from Lowrys isn't noticed very much because he lets the other man do the talking; however, he breaks his silence every now and then with sharp flashes of wit. He is a true and congenial companion, and anyone in trouble can always depend upon "J. H." to do his best to help him. "J. H." is an ardent disciple of "Crip" and "Pete". He enlisted in the pursuit of the minute electron four years ago, and if he keeps up his good work he is sure to catch the little gentleman some day. Hardee is a hard and earnest worker, and if he follows his work as he is capable of doing, he is sure to mount high in the electrical engineering business. Here's luck to you, "J. H.".

name: Haskell, Alexander Cheves Jr.
motto: Fear and trust in the Lord, and He will lead thee through life.
clubs: R. O. T. C. Plattsburg Barracks, N. Y.
description: Alexander Cheves Haskell, Jr., known to the Corps as "Hoss", lives near the banks of the Savannah River just far enough out of Georgia to be a valued citizen of the Palmetto State. Alex, a disciple of the well known "Hoss" Mullins, followed in that man's footsteps, but he took such a short pace that he didn't get any further than a captain. With that old "Plowboy stride" of his, Alex kept his place on the cross-country team and track squad for the four years that he spent at Tigertown and by using his "block" and his legs he won his "Block" in the early stages of the game. Alex is free from all entanglements of the fairer sex and it is said that the only maiden that ever caused him to look twice happened to be of the bovine species. Some girl for some unknown reason wrote the boy a letter one time. It was three days before he mastered enough courage to open it and then in the excitement he missed three classes. Alex has made his mark at Clemson and has made it well.

name: Hayden, Edward Copeland
motto: Associate with men of good quality if you esteem your own reputation; for it is better to be alone than in bad company.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: In September, nineteen twenty-three, "Cope" bid for a berth in the Tiger's lair with the Class of '27. While Clemson has heartily welcomed her gain in capturing this man, his home town grieved over the great loss. "Cope" is a loyal, hard working Tiger; for his efforts he has been rewarded with a host of friends. He is not thought to be a sheik, but by the vast number of the fair sex clinging to his string, we would not expect him to be a bachelor very long. To know "Cope" is to like him. He is a pal, a friend, and a classmate never to be forgotten. We are sure that "Cope" will succeed in any kind of work he chooses. So, "Cope", we all wish you success and happiness.

name: Hayden, Thomas Jefferson
motto: He only is exempt from failure who makes no effort.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: In the fall of 1923, T. J., as he is known by all of his friends in school, decided to leave the metropolis of Rowesville, S. C. and journey to Tigertown. He came in search of that elusive thing known as higher learning. After a long and careful consideration, T. J. cast his lot with the Civil Engineers, and since that time has spent many an afternoon surveying the hills and dales around Clemson. Besides being an excellent student, T. J. is also a regular fellow—one who cannot be overlooked. His cheerful attitude toward life, his genteel manner, and his willingness to lend a helping hand win for him a host of friends wherever he goes. We sincerely hope, T. J., that success and happiness will be your lot in life, and that you'll soon win some fair little girl to share them with you.

name: Hendee, Malcolm Henry Jr.
motto: Know thyself, and to thyself be true.
clubs: Rifle Marksmanship, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: When a human being is created, a mold of his character is passed among the "Gods of Traits," and each places in the cast such an endowment as he sees fit. Loyalty and Determination dealt generously with "Tick". His loyalty is a by-word among his friends; for once you have gained his friendship it is now and forever with him. He doesn't give his friendship lightly, and he never takes it back. By his determined efforts "Tick" has gained a distinction that is perhaps enjoyed only by himself. He has represented Clemson in every branch of sport at some time during his school career. The unusualness of this record is made evident by the fact that very few men are able to participate even in the major sports. Wishing "Tick" success would be but a matter of form for he never fails; but we do wish for him sincere happiness in succeeding.

name: Hendrix, Theron Judson
motto: Speech is the mirror of the soul; as a man speaks so is he.
clubs: Senior R.O.T.C., Plattsburg Barracks, N. Y.
description: This Tiger came to us from the "Peach Center", Greer, S. C. Theron came from the farm to Clemson but thinking that he would not like the "life of a farmer" decided to cast his lot with the C. E.'s. He is a member of the A. S. C. E., a distinguished society for Civil Engineers who make a satisfactory record in class work. Hendrix is a man of little talk and much thought; he never says anything unless he means it, and you can always depend upon what he says. Theron, when you get that little girl that you are continually writing to and get settled, we predict nothing but success for you. The Class of '27 wishes you well in life.

name: Herron, James Levis
motto: Live not to please yourself, but to please others.
description: "Runt" came to Clemson from the town of Starr. Since his arrival here in the fall of 1923, he has been working steadily as a student and as an athlete. On the baseball diamond he has thrown fear into the hearts of opposing teams; his hitting has helped win many a victory. After two years of hard study, "Runt" decided to cast his lot with the Animal Husbandry section. His quickness of perception and keenness of mind has enabled him to make good as a student. This lad is a true friend and a loyal Tiger. If the qualities of honesty, frankness, fearlessness, and perseverance pave the way for success "Runt" should make good.

name: Hicks, Montague Hoskins
motto: Smile and the world smiles with you.
clubs: Captain Co. "B" Football team, Winner of Regiment Trophy '26
Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: "Monty", as he is known by his admirers in College, is better known to the fair sex as Montague. He won fame with the ladies, his marvelous success being partly due to the nature of being a "Woman hater". That isn't anything unusual because most of us are sooner or later inclined to be that way. "Monty" decided to expose himself to Electricity because someone told him his head would make a good dry-cell, and that this would be a great assistance to him in the future. His record as a student has not been the best, but he is now ready to begin his life as a graduate. Luck to you, "Monty", a true friend and a loyal Tiger. We wish you the greatest of success.

name: Hinnant, James Linwood
motto: To say little and perform much shows the characteristics of a great mind.
description: "All the world loves a lover", so we all love Jimmie. This lad hails from the Capital City, and for four years he has been breaking hearts in our neighboring cities. As a sheik, Jimmie has no equal—ask the Anniston, Ala., girls. Jimmie is a tireless worker. His diligence in the realm of mechanical engineering has won for him the cognomen of "Entropy" Hinnant. This would lead one to think he is a Mechanical Engineer, but far from this; for he is an Electrical Engineer of the highest order. This boy's rare combination of personality, intellect, and industry will win recognition from the Electrical world. "A good man can't be kept down", so the sky is the limit for you, Jimmie.

name: Hutto, Stanwix Greenville
motto: Think twice before you speak, then whisper.
description: When the clamor and tumult of four years of varied experiences have rolled by, and when their effects have been printed upon the faces and instituted in the bearings of the members of the Class of '27, there will be one if no more retaining his pleasantness and unique personality. And that one is our. Through the crises and crests of his college career he has manfully challenged failure to a mortal combat. He has at last emerged from the fray a victor. This boy has gained a name for himself. Pleasant of disposition, smiling and carefree when gloom was prevailing elsewhere, kindly of heart, open to convictions, admitting his errors--these are a few of his qualities. A true specimen of affability of nature is presented to the world by this same. When the load of life's responsibilities presses upon his shoulders, there is no fear as to his plan of action. With his store of potentiality, he will forge ahead. Hutto, we are looking to you for your best.

name: James, John H.
motto: Listen to every man's advice, but back your own judgment.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This lad came to Clemson from Greer, the "Peach Center" of South Carolina. John H., as he is known to his friends--and his friends are numbered by his acquaintances--is a person of high character and ability. John H. has made an excellent record in his class work during his college life. We predict nothing but success for a person of such determination and ability. Johnnie is a good "mixer" and we are predicting a successful career for him in his chosen profession of Agricultural Education. Luck to you, Johnnie. The best wishes of the Class of '27 go with you.

name: Johnson, William Perry
motto: Prove thyself a man, be the circumstances as they may.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Plattsburg Barracks, New York
description: No, dear friends, this is not a Listerine advertisement, but a portion of the map of one "Ohms" Johnson. The insidious thing about it is that a picture cannot portray his masculine beauty. Perry came to Clemson with the full intention of revolutionizing the electrical industry, and making Inman the Electrical City of the South. After carefully following the Electrical Engineering curriculum for three years, he suddenly decided to become a literary genius, and is now an ardent admirer of "D. W." and Mussolini. He has become so radical that he has started wearing supporters, and it is rumored that he will soon be wearing spats. "W. P." has won many friends with his cheerful disposition. He is studious but knows how to combine joy with work, and is always ready to take part in a "bull" conference. Johnson is bound to make a success. His friends wish him the best of luck.

name: Jones, Oma Franklin
motto: What a man holds behind his teeth hurts neither himself nor anyone else.
clubs: Pickens County Club, Secretary and Treasurer, '26
Sharpshooter, Machine Gun, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala
description: Oma has made for himself a host of friends at Clemson—in fact, everyone cherishes the idea of knowing him and of being his friend. He possesses the qualities that automatically make one like him, and it is through those qualities that he acquired the love, respect, and admiration of the Class of '27. For some reason or other Oma decided that he'd be a Professor, and he has the distinction of being a worthy member of "Sooky's" outfit. It is rumored that a certain Winthrop Senior has already applied for a position in his school, and they say that they have had it planned for a number of years. We can now see his idea in being a "Prof". With his determination to succeed, and his bull dog tenacity to do the right thing, we know that Oma is bound to succeed. Remember, Oma, that the Class of '27 wishes you luck in whatever you may undertake.

name: Kershaw, John
motto: What I must do concerns me—not what people think.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: "Krush" lives on the banks of the Savannah, and when asked where he is from, he says, "Augusta," but heartily adds that that only identifies him. John's quiet, unassuming manner has won for him a warm place in the hearts of all who know him. He is not a brilliant student, but is steady and conscientious, and has done what he has thought best regardless of the consequences. Here is to you, "Krush". May all of your troubles be little ones with blue eyes and black hair.

name: Keyserling, Leonard Levy
motto: Live and let live.
description: It was in the fall of '25 when "Kiz" left the wide open spaces of Beaufort for the jungles of the Seneca. He first attended Ga. Tech. for two years, but by that time he realized that to Clemson he must come, if he were to be a real electrical engineer; and it is here that he has thrived on "juice". Always striving hard in whatever he undertakes, "Kiz" has been successful in his chosen field. A bright future lies before him if he tackles life with the same determination that has marked everything he has done. Although with us only two years, he has won himself a wide circle of friends. He carries with him the best wishes of the Class of '27, and there will be many eyes to watch him make his mark in the world.

name: Kitchen, Thomas Ward
motto: Let nothing discourage you; never give up.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Here is to one of the most worthy lads that Greenville has ever given to the Tigers. Tom came to Clemson in the fall of '23 and cast his lot with the Textiles. He has been very successful in this course and we all predict a great future for him. However, everyone is afraid it won't be for him alone. He has very important business in Greenville almost every week-end, but as much as he thinks of this business, he also thinks of his work. He is a good sport, a true friend, and a gentleman. Tom, the Class of '27 wishes for you the greatest success in whatever you undertake. Go to it.

name: Knight, James Donald
motto: I often regret my speech, but never my silence.
description: Following in the footsteps of his brother, "J. D." decided to seek his higher education at Clemson. He entered in the fall of 1923, and has done his bit in fighting the battles of college life ever since then. He, having as his ambition to be a second Edison, chose Electrical Engineering as his course, and has very earnestly applied himself to such, as is evidenced by his high-class standing. While he is by no means a sheik, his man does at times turn to the fairer sex. This may be attested by the many letters which he receives from Alexander and elsewhere. My prophecy is that he will never be a member of any bachelor society. "J. D." is a true friend and a gentleman. A man that you have to know in order to fully appreciate. Here's to you, my lad. We are expecting you to make a record for yourself and Clemson.

name: Landlubber, Silver McPirate
clubs: Quarterback Seneca Valley Sunday Football Team '49, '50
Charter Member and Organizer Jailbird's Club
Office Boy, Secretary, General (Nuisance) of Cutthroat's Club
Founder of Game Commonly Known as "Treasure Hunt"
Backstop and Waterboy Baseball Team of '50
description: Back in the fall of '49, when men were men and murder a duty, this stately lad hove into Clemson from Santuc. As he passed through the market section of Sandy Springs, he halted his giraffe at the sight of an "Adventure" magazine—Ah, for adventure was something he had heard of. He mounted his steed and sailed on to Tigertown. The lure of the story proved fatal. As he passed the gates, he met a famous Danish Prince, Ophelia's Blowberg. In a flash young Silver drew his slingshot, relieved his captive of a chew of licorice—and was away in a flash. The Discipline Committee met. Ere the sun sank Landlubber had bartered his fiery steed of popular characteristics for a man-o'-war constructed of selected cornstalks. Away he glided down the traditional Seneca, off for a career of blood. Years have passed, lives to him have been as mushrooms, things to be stepped on. But now old age has come, with its aches and ills, and other qualms; so Silver has returned to be revived by the Tiger spirit, and to graduate with the Class of '27.

name: Lee, Samuel Austin
motto: Better trust all and be deceived, And weep that trust and that deceiving Than doubt one heart that if believed Had blest one's life with true believing.
clubs: Sharpshooter Howitzer Company R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan Alabama
description: "Snake" is the personification of all that goes to make a regular fellow. His cheerfulness, wit, and willingness to help everyone, have earned numerous friends for him. Always smiling and congenial—that's Sam all over. All these attributes of charm have naturally attracted the ladies, and "Snake" has a new "line" for every girl he meets. Sam doesn't spend all of his time with the ladies, as his class records will testify. He is one of the outstanding men in the electrical section, and will some day discover why electricity is so shocking. When he leaves school and enters his chosen profession, he is sure to write his name in big letters upon the topmost pinnacle of success. Although he is not an original member of the Class of '27, the class is glad to have him as a member of its honored group. "Snake," your numerous friends wish you the best. When you face the world to fight life's battles—remember that '27 is pulling for you.

name: Sherwood, Edmond Liles Jr.
motto: The only way to have a friend is to be one.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Above our life we love a steadfast friend--that is why we all love Sam. Of a quiet type that never pushes his way by extra effort among his associates or to positions of high honor, he possesses an enriched and ennobled gift of diligently working his way to the top and winning the highest esteem of those about him. Sam would do nothing to blight the happiness of his friends. He would do just opposite. It is not his fault that he unconsciously captivates the hearts of the girls claimed by his friends, but it is due to his captivating manner and pleasing personality. But these things never cross the path of duty of Sam for there is no other more capable and efficient. It is said that every man has his gift and the tools go to him that can use them. Truly he has used his tools for his many accomplishments, characterized by nobility of purpose, have always been attained by patience and perseverance even though it has never been necessary for him to "crack" a book. Along with this Sam possesses the qualities of a true gentleman, and he may be relied on as a faithful friend, "Ming et Semper".

name: Lenhardt, Benjamin Franklin
motto: Deal square with every one, and be a friend to God and man.
description: Clemson's stock ran high in the fall of '23 when the town of Easley paid her debt to this state institution. Ben began his college career by taking Agriculture. To know Ben is to be acquainted with a good fellow and a true gentleman. A big heart, a generous disposition, and an eagerness to help his friends, have won for him a host of friends at Clemson. He is one of our most popular members of the Class of '27, and it would be incomplete without him. Ben has never claimed to be a ladies' man but his frequent visits to the P. O. indicate that there is a certain young lady who is anxious for June to come. So here's to you, "Ben". We wish you success, and may you some day derive the highest degree of happiness in the cozy little cabin of your dreams.

name: Starr, S. C.
motto: To thine own self be true.
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C. Camp McClellan, Ala
description: Bob's pleasing smile and helping hand have caused a large number of his classmates to see the bright and better side of life. His unselfishness and true gentlemanly sportsmanship have gained him a place in the hearts of all who know him. It is impossible to look into Bob's future; he makes you too well pleased with the present. From all indications he has something on his mind that is not found in Civil Engineering. Bob, the four years you have spent here have been a success and we feel sure it shall be the same after you leave us. Our wishes are for you.

name: Ernest, McClain Long
motto: It can be done.
description: This lad, with his pleasing and unassuming personality, has made many staunch friends while sojourning at "Tigertown". Lighthearted, full of life, never worried about the future, keen of perception, impartial in opinions, and delightfully frank—these are a few of the gifted traits of character which make "E.M." a congenial companion, a true friend, an able student, and above all, an honorable gentleman. Here's to you, "E. M." May Luck, success, and happiness be yours. The Class of '27 will cherish your memory.

name: McCRACKEN, Henry Emmett
motto: For age and want save while you may; no morning sun lasts a whole day.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
Judging Team to International Live Stock Show at Chicago '27
description: "Mac", as we all know him, is a quiet, consistent, and very progressive student; which facts are proved by the enviable scholastic record made by him while at Clemson. The ability which is his will be sure to bring him success in future years. To know "Mac" is to like him because he is of the highest type of gentlemen. He insists upon that which is right, says little, and sticks to what he says. His honesty, truthfulness, and loyalty go to make up his greatest assets. By his congenial, polite nature he has won the love, respect, and admiration of the entire corps.

name: McDANIEL, Sim William
motto: Knowledge is the only fountain, both of the love and principles of human interest.
description: "Mac" is one of those lads who get along so well in the world because they have that admirable quality of being able to mind their own business. Never pushing himself forward but always content to take things as they come, he has won for himself quite a host of friends during his four years at Clemson. He takes part in all social activities, and has several positions of honor and leadership.
