Although "Mac" has never made a block letter he has taken a great deal of interest in athletics, and on whatever team you find him his presence is strongly felt by the opponents. Entering college with the determination to be an architect, his sense of duty and nobility of purpose have carried him well to the front of his class. We predict for him the same success in future years.

name: McKemie, William Joel
motto: After the verb 'To Love', 'To Help' is the most beautiful in the world.
description: Did not join our class until his Junior year. He came to our ranks from Georgia Tech from which he received his nick-name. Prior to this he finished at Tech. High School in Atlanta. Since deserting the "Yellow Jackets" and joining the "Tigers" he has shown up well in basketball, tennis, cross-country and, last but not least, in "bobbin-dogging". Bill has acquired two bad habits during his stay at Clemson. He smokes "Camel" cigarettes and writes too often to a certain young lady in Atlanta. Although he has been with us only two years he has a host of friends. He believes that the way to have friends is to be a friend. Bill is a likable, hard-working, earnest student who meets every obstacle with a smile. If he had his personality alone he would have a fortune. We wish you success, "Bill", in anything that you undertake.

name: McKeown, Samuel Moffatt
motto: God and the world loves a fighter not the kind that will bravely lie down and die, but the kind that will fight like hell to live.
clubs: Chester County Club, Secretary and Treasurer '25
Senior R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: That "Mac" has led a widely active life since he has been a student at Clemson is not more than half manifested by a glance at the list of honors, of which he may well be proud. One can hardly mention any branch of college activities presented at Clemson in which "Wedge" has not been a participant, and he has acquitted himself with credit in every phase. Too, the scope of his knowledge permits him to converse on almost any topic, thus making him a peculiar combination of salesman, promoter, lecturer, and go-getter. Can there be a limit to a fortune for one so versatile?

name: McLeod, Claude Eugene
motto: Have more than thou showest; know more than thou sayest.
description: Desirous of learning more of the intricacies of plant life, he left his native heath and came to Clemson to cast his lot with the Class of '27, and to delve into the mysteries of Horticulture. Always hardworking and earnest, he has achieved success in his course and though modest and retiring, has won for himself a multitude of friends. He has a winning personality, and in addition to this trait, he possesses that rare quality of inspiring the confidence of others. As captain of the track team he has proved an able and inspiring leader. He will probably return to his native Beaufort to put into practice some of his pet theories about truck farming. Wherever he may go, he carries with him the best wishes of the Class of '27 for much success in this work, and with the "one" and "only" girl about whom he so rarely, but convincingly, speaks.

name: McMillan, Francis Williamson
motto: There is none so deaf as he who will not hear.
description: In the fall of 1923, he arrived at Clemson with the idea of becoming a "Lint-head". He has acquired great skill in the last four years, and we know he will make a success in the Textile World. Although small in stature, he is a big-hearted fellow who never troubles trouble. He is a good sport, ready to give or take, never grouchy, but always smiling. He has many friends because of the fact that he will stand by one through thick and thin. Winning the hearts of the ladies has been one of his side hobbies, and from all appearances he has succeeded well. Life holds a great many fine things for you. We predict for you a great future. May success be yours in whatever you undertake to do.

name: McTeer, James Raymond
motto: We need no law, no jury or no judge to tell us when we're wrong.
clubs: Sharpshooter, Howitzer Company, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Mc", as he is called, hails from Walterboro, one of those low country towns around which stand giant water oaks draped with Spanish moss. "Mc" brought this air of romance to Clemson with him in the fall of '23. At first he was very shy with the ladies, but just before the State Fair of his Senior year, "Mc" began to "step out". Since that time he has become quite a ladies' man. "Mc" is a hard worker, and is straightforward in his dealings with his fellowmen. To say that he is a "prince" of a fellow would be putting it rather mildly. He has a good nature and a sense of humor. "Mc" is one of "Crip's" Electricals who will make the Class of '06 look. Here's to you, "Mc", may success attend your every effort.

name: Major, James Dexter
motto: He who fears ridicule is at the mercy of every fool.
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: There comes a time in the course of human events when a youth finishes high school and wonders, "Where now?" Such was not true of "Jake". Two or three years before the fall of '23 he decided to enter Clemson with the full expectation of learning more about the processes of Agriculture. During his stay with us he has been known for his "witty" sayings, his ability in the classroom, and his quiet, peaceful manner. "Jake" has little to say, but when he speaks everybody listens for they know there is something going to be said. "Jake" is a lover of nature, and probably that is one reason for his casting his lot with the "Aggies" and becoming a disciple of "Lord" Collings. He likes to spend his spare time in the woods with his gun ready to capture any game that appears. He claims that he likes his gun better than he does the girls, but we are not certain about that. "Jake" is known to us as a man of fair play. What more could we request? Although a man small in stature, he has a big heart and will listen to reason at all times. He justly deserves much for his sterling character, mental worth, and natural ability. Briefly, it may be said of him that he is a gentleman and a scholar.

name: Marshall, Richard Maynard Jr.
motto: My secret wishes would my choice decide; But open justice bends to neither side.
clubs: Charter Member of the Sabre Club
American Red Cross Life Saving Corps
description: Having spent part of his grammar school days on the campus, Maynard early caught the real Tiger Spirit. He first attended Union College in Schenectady, but as this did not afford an opportunity for his military genius to blossom forth, he returned to Clemson the following year. Here he has proved himself to be a true disciple of "Pistol Pete," and has won the distinction of being "King Cole's" right-hand man. It is quite natural that he should have chosen Electrical Engineering as his major course, for since boyhood he has been tinkering with this mysterious form of energy. In days to come it would not surprise us to hear of him as the second Steinmetz. Maynard, here's wishing for you a bright and happy future, and may you always live up to the ideals of your Alma Mater.

name: Massey, Larry Brewington
motto: The past is a memory; the present is a fact; the future is hope.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: It has been the pleasure of the Class of '27 to have as one of its members a cheerful and very congenial lad who hails from the neighboring city of Pendleton. This member, who is none other than Larry B. Massey, has made many friends during his four years at Clemson due primarily to his fine personality, and his witty and varied remarks that he has for all those with whom he comes in contact. Larry, as he is known, came to us with an excellent record from high school, and has continued to add to that record since he came to Clemson. It is with regret that we, the Class of '27, have to part with Larry, but we are consoled by the fact that we know what the future holds in store for a fellow who has the determination that Larry has--nothing short of success. Luck to you, Larry, the Class of '27 wishes you well in life.

name: Warren Candler Maxwell
motto: Work conquers all.
clubs: Secretary, President, The Palmetto Literary Society
description: In the fall of 1923 Georgia lent South Carolina one of her worthy native sons to the end that South Carolina's halls of learning might afford the development of a latent leadership. Both states have profited by this transaction, and it was with a spirit of welcome that Clemson received Warren Maxwell into her midst. Naturally and by application Warren is a leader. His activities cover most of the phases of college life; and whenever he chose to direct his efforts along a particular line, leadership in that activity was inevitably attained. Warren is an outstanding student in religious activities and literary society work, and his scholastic record is not paralleled. Cheerfulness, friendliness, courteousness, and similar virtues may be aptly used to describe Warren, for in him we find the elements of character so mixed that the Class of 1927 might stand out to all the world and say, "This is a Christian gentleman!" Here's to you, Warren! May your success continue.

name: Paul Francis Meredith
motto: Honi soit qui mal y pense.
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: The name plate which appears in connection with the above "sap" has long since ceased to be representative of the facial terrain for which it was dedicated. This advertisement is better known to its friends at Clemson and Camp McClellan as "Droop". A glance at "Droop's" military honors will convince anyone that he is a soldier second only to Napoleon. "Droop" first had an ambition to become a man of literary attainments, but after a stay of a year at Carolina he abandoned the idea and departed for Clemson to prove his engineering ability. His inability to adapt himself to the military life at Clemson was the cause of his taking two extended vacations. However, "Droop" came back in '25 and resolved that he had sown all of his wild oats, and now everyone is proud of him and the work which he has done in all phases of college life. To his friends, "Droop" is known as a man of sterling character, strong in his convictions, but with it all a prince of a fellow. A gentleman and a scholar is Meredith.

name: Metz, Gustave Ernest
motto: "Then let us fill this little interval, this pause of life, with all the virtue we can crowd into it."
clubs: Corresponding Secretary, Treasurer, The Palmetto Literary Society
description: Gloom came over the City of Charleston in the fall of '23. One of its illustrious sons was leaving. Gus chose to cast his lot with the "plough-boys," but after one and a half years in this course, he decided that his ambitions did not run along agricultural lines. Accordingly, he changed his course. In General Science and Education he found the work he liked and has applied himself diligently to this work since then. Considering both quantity and quality of work, he probably has the best record in his class. He has also taken active part in religious work and other outside activities, and is known as a leader in those in which he takes part. Gus is a true friend, a real gentleman, and a loyal Christian—a man anyone would do well to know. Here's to you, Gus! We are looking for you to accomplish great things in educational reform.

name: Miley, Percy
description: Percy came to Clemson in the fall of '23 to complete his education, casting his lot with the "Horticultural Group." He had not been here long before making many true friends especially among the fairer sex. Percy has a pleasing personality which accounts for the horde of friends he has made at Clemson. He is conscientious and a hard worker, which has given him a high place in the esteem of his professors and associates. From the frequent trips he makes to the nearby metropolis of Greenville, we predict that he will soon be lost in matrimony. Percy, the Class of '27 wishes you the best of luck in all of your undertakings.

name: Loudwin, Rogers Miller
motto: To thine own self be true, and it must follow, as night the day, thou canst not then be false to any man.
clubs: Machine Gun Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Take and mix well equal parts of friendship, good fellowship, honor, ability, and sunshine and you will have an imitation of this child from Bennettsville; but at that it will be only a fair one for there is, and can be, on this mortal coil only one like "Urgis". With a vision of whirring motors and polished, throbbing engines, the slaves of the great god Power, this lad came to Clemson to learn to be a loyal subject of this all-powerful genius. And he has got a darned good start. He will find his way to promotion and pay; so learn about men from him! Rogers has taken his fun where he found it and while so doing has made an enviable reputation which cannot be equalled by the best of Clemson men; hence he will not be forgotten! As he leaves old Tigertown in search of the elusive electron and the evasive B. T. U. he carries with him the love, respect, and best wishes of the entire Tiger pack.

name: Philip, Hudson Miller
motto: Everywhere in life the true question is not what we gain, but what we do.
clubs: Machine Gun Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Suavely pursuing his courses, accomplishing his tasks with a manipulation of his talents that, in resemblance, is like unto that use of tools which the skilled artisan employs, "Judge" has gained the ardent admiration of those who have worked and played with him. With his ready smile and optimistic attitude, along with other admirable traits he has gained a host of friends. Coming to his Alma Mater with noble intentions, conducting himself in such a manner as to bring honor to himself and his school, he has reached the heights of Success. He carries into the world of tomorrow, into which he is soon to embark, the ideals of a true gentleman. We can predict nothing other than Success there. So here's to you, "Judge"—may you never fail us.

name: Milling, James Alexander
motto: Of what shall a man be proud, if he is not proud of his friends.
clubs: Captain R.O.T.C., Championship Team Camp McClellan, Ala. '26
Member Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Once a friend, always a friend, and "Shine" is always ready to be a friend to anyone. With "Shine," a man's wealth lies not in his money but in his friends. It is a pleasure to be in his company, for you are immediately imbued with a spirit of optimism. His winning personality and good disposition are like a magnet, and not a few fair ladies are proud to be called "one of 'Shine's' girls." His influence at Clemson has ever been prevailing, and many a time as Cheer Leader, when the "Tigers" were confronted by defeat, by his undaunted spirit of loyalty to his Alma Mater, he raised the spirit of the students and team from its lowest ebb to cause them to push on to victory. As an executive, he may be judged by the honors which his classmates have entrusted with him as a natural-born leader of men. By saying it all in just a few words, "Shine" is "Shine, now and forever"; he will be recognized by everyone who has had the good fortune of being affected by his rays of friendship.

name: Mitchell, Roy Herman
motto: I would look up, and love, and laugh, and lift.
clubs: Member of Winning Sprint Medley Team at Tech Relays
Sergeant-at-Arms, Recording Secretary, Term President of Calhoun Literary Society
Member Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
Sect. and Treas., Sophomore Dancing Club
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Red" must succeed, for he is a gentleman by birth and a scholar by acquisition. His irrepressible good nature and engaging manners have gained for him boundless admiration. Always aflame with life and glowing with happiness, he is delightful, witty and sensible. He is known by his hilarious laugh and intoxicating smile, and is not stingy with either. He is the life of every party, but is always retaining the correct proportion of reserve and dignity; and by his laudable enthusiasm in everything, he is always welcomed. One must go a long way to find another who is always in such perfect good humor. Merging wit with wisdom, he is active and aggressive. His cares and worries are hidden behind a veil of cheerfulness, and secretly they are well attended.

name: Newman, Charles Carter Jr.
motto: No one may rest on his laurels.
clubs: Holder of Clemson, State, and Southern Conference records one-half Mile
Southeastern A. A. U. Half-Mile Champion '23, '24
Southern Champion Sprint Medley Relay team '26
mile and half-mile Champion Fourth Corps Area '26
Fourth Area Championship Mile Relay Team '26
Competitor National Inter Collegiate Track Championships, Chicago '26
Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Being born and reared in the shadow of Clemson's revered walls, Carter is a Tiger by heredity and by choice, and to this dual relationship together with his personality and determination can be accredited the wonderful success he has made of being a Tiger. During the four years that he has spent at college he has run many races, won many medals, broken records, and captured honors. Although no book worm, he has held his own with the pedagogues, but his "long suit" is outside activities. He can be truly called the outstanding athlete of the Class of '27. When his class calls for leaders, Carter's name is always mentioned. He has held honors—state, national, and local—of which he is to be proud, but to be valued above all these are the friendships he has made.

name: Nichols, Patrick Mark
motto: Study as if you were to live forever, live as if you were to die tomorrow.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Newberry County Club '26-'27
description: "Nick" hails from the metropolis of Silver Street. Having cast his lot with the Tigers in the fall of '22, he let his conscience be his guide and left for Newberry College. He stayed there for three years, and returned to Clemson in '25. He followed in pursuit of Electrical Engineering for one year. After a year he changed to the Academic course. "Nick" having been here only two years has become a true Tiger. He is determined to pilot the good ship "Success" through the rough waters of life. So here's to you, "Nick"; we wish you the best success possible. We know you will succeed in your life's work, including the M. R. S. degree.

name: Nickles, William David
motto: You can tell what kind of wheels a man has in his head by the 'spokes' that come out of his mouth.
clubs: Member Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: What more can be said of a man than that he is a loyal "Tiger" and a true friend to all? These are outstanding characteristics of "Bill". He came to us that never-to-be-forgotten September day, four years ago, wearing the same winning smile that has won him so many friends. He may be small in stature, but his heart makes up for this. "Bill" cast his lot with the electrical engineers, and for the last four years has been chasing those electrons Steinmetz missed. He is a hard worker, not only in his classes, but also in everything he undertakes, and he is certain to make a success. We all wish to you, Bill, both success and "Bee", and everything that goes with these.

name: Page, William
motto: Words are like leaves; and where they most abound, Much fruit of sense is rarely found.
clubs: President and Secretary Horry County Club
description: "Bill" entered Clemson in the fall of '23. He claims the "Independent Republic of Horry" as his native home. "Bill" is a lad of few words, but words full of mirth and truth. He is very quiet and unassuming—a true gentleman of the highest calibre. He has wasted but few moments on the fairer sex while at Clemson, thinking perhaps that women and Prof. Crandall's Education would not mix; however, we predict that some school will be greatly benefited by his presence as a teacher, and that some girl will be made happy when he "pops the question". Here's hoping your County and State will realize your ability as we do, and that you may never rest until success has been yours.

name: Parler, Marvin Lamar Jr.
motto: Hang sorrow. Care will kill a cat, and therefore, let's be merry.
clubs: Architectural Society, Vice-President '26, Secretary and Treasurer '25
Sumter County Club, Vice-President '27, Secretary and Treasurer '26
Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This lad possesses those innumerable qualities which go to make up a true scholar and a gentleman. He is one of the few who never have to study but who always stand at the head of the class. He has a personality which acts as a magnet in attracting to him the trust and confidence of all who know him. He is an efficient executive and has held many positions of prominence here. Not over-bearing or forward, he is always good natured and smiling, and makes friends wherever he goes. He never allows his joviality to interfere with his duty, however. He is always ready to perform any task that comes his way, and performs it well. He has helped make many social functions a huge success. We predict for him a great success in life.

name: Parnell, Charlie Leonadus
motto: It is a mistake to suppose that men succeed through success. They much oftener succeed through failure.
description: Charlie hails from the level stretches of sand swamps where huge, stately live oaks and silent, hanging moss lend a somber charm and an impelling beauty to the country. Making this his base of operation, he has sallied out at intervals intent on securing some fair trophy of the hills. But by no means has this been the dominating motive of his pilgrimage to Clemson. Charlie has an intense desire for knowledge. Trustworthy and dependable, he merits the utmost confidence of his friends and associates. He is an earnest student and a hard worker. His utter frankness and sincerity have won him many friends. Your loyalty to friends and your determination have stamped you as a success.

name: Phillips, Charlie Young
motto: The moment past is no longer; the future may be; the present is all of which man is master.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: "Cyrus" came to Clemson with the determination to win. Throughout his course he has shown the qualities of perseverance and hard work. "Cy" shows that a real man can succeed because in the face of adverse circumstances he has remained with his original class. "Cy" has not broken any records in athletics or flourished as a military genius, but he is always present when the glee club goes into action. He has worked hard to "make the club", and deserves much credit. The nickname "Songbird" was bestowed upon him by some admirer. Condensed into as few words as possible, that which characterizes him is the spirit of "never say die". "Cy's" many friends wish him great success in his chosen field of work.

name: Pickelsimer, Dennis Leo
motto: Be what your friends think you are and not what your enemies say you are.
description: "Pick" is not the seeker of popularity, so commonly found in such a large body of men. On the other hand, he is the reserved true blue that is to be most desired in a friend. He is delightfully frank, yet tactful enough, and with that so sought for quality of being understood. This 220 pounds of solid man has been cruelly deprived of a permanent seat on the football team by a bad knee. Yet, despite his bulk he is active on the diamond and graceful on the basketball floor. We have vainly hoped that this malady would soon be cured. Nobody knows whether or not "Pick" can handle the ladies. He has never tried. We think that he is a confirmed woman-hater. Yet by the time the glories and beauties of the success that is sure to come are laid at his feet, he will, no doubt, have been captivated by one of them.

name: Pruitt, William Hanks
motto: Better than fame or applause is striving to further a cause.
description: He was a natural lover of animals of the farm, and thus cast his lot with the "Ags," later joining the "Bull" Division. His goal has not been to attain military or athletic honors, but to know good animals when he sees them. He decided what he wanted, and ever since has been striving diligently toward that end. He has made good in the classroom, and has formed a host of friends. His success as a student is to be contributed to his natural ability coupled with a willingness to work and a desire to make good. We wish you the best of luck. Work in the world as you have at Clemson and success will be yours.

name: Rasor, Henry Latimore
motto: One thing is forever good; that one thing is success.
description: Will you cast your eyes upward and look into the face of a boy who stands high in his class work as well as in stature? This is probably due to the crowded environment of Donalds. At first, Rasor found it very difficult to become accustomed to the quiet life at Clemson, but in a short while he became a tame Tiger. After two years at Clemson, Rasor became a disciple of "Sookie", and since then has put forth much effort in his desire to become a professor. We have been unable to find out much about this lad's love affairs, but we believe that there are a few things which he has kept from us. We, the Class of '27, are proud to call you one of our classmates. May you always be successful in making friends as you have been while at Clemson.

name: Reed, Lucius Brown
motto: Always do what is right, let come what will.
clubs: Active Member of Y. M. C. A.
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Some Entomologists are born while others are made. We would conclude that Lucius was made an Entomologist as he spent two years in College before making up his mind to take "Bugology". But never can it be said that he wasted any of his four years at Clemson for he chased away every doubt with a determination to learn entomology in all of its phases. Knowledge so gained "sticks" and is the keynote to real success in whatever path you may choose to tread. Now that you have struck the right note may it be yours to follow its worthy sound and achieve the success we covet for all "Loyal Clemson Tigers".

name: Reynolds, Clifford Marion
motto: Never worry.
clubs: R. O. T. C. Tennis Team
description: Hail the "Mighty Pedro", the conqueror from the great metropolis of Bennettsville. A man among men, is this lad, and a terror among ladies. "Pete's" first venture in the sea of knowledge was to join the ranks of Electricals. At the close of his Junior year he decided that he had missed his calling; so he became a charter member of Prof. Brearley's Academic Course. The fact that he was president of the Calhoun Literary Society showed that he was well fitted for this course. No one could wish for a better friend than "Pete". To say that he is a "prince of a fellow" would be putting it too mildly. His attractive personality, his good nature, and his willingness to sacrifice for others have made for him a host of true friends at Clemson.

name: Rickborn, James Hammond
motto: True worth is being, not seeming.
clubs: Member Bamberg County Club '26, Vice President '27
Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: The Gods surely smiled on "Rick" when he was born, for only in this manner may a man be endowed with all the admirable traits which he possesses. He is first and last a gentleman. His congeniality, squareness, and above all his personality, have earned him friends by the score. "Rick" started making friends when he was a "Rat," and now in his Senior year his friends at "Tigertown" include not only the cadets, but also the members of the faculty with whom he has been associated. Clemson has turned out many an admirable man; but never has she turned out one who can excel "Rick" as a good fellow. "J. H." is majoring in Agronomy, and his ability coupled with his willingness to work is sure to make him a success in his chosen work.

name: Robinson, Oscar Stokes
motto: Be what you are.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This dark-eyed lad is none other than "Robbie," sometimes called "Dur-Bur," because that is his favorite and only "Cuss" word. He hails from the historic old town of York, from whence a large number of Tigers have come. His motto should be, "Be what you are," as he has always been open-hearted and loyal to his friends. He is well known by his ready smile and winning personality. Although small in stature he is not afraid of trouble. This is evidenced by the fact that he chose Electrical Engineering as his profession. He has been a faithful disciple of "Slim," and one of his chief joys was making Entropy Charts for "Red," while "Spark Plug" says that he certainly would like to retain Robbie as assistant instructor in machine-shop. We all feel sure that if he can only manage to keep away from Columbia, he will make a great success and be a credit to his Alma Mater.

name: Rogers, Clarence Milford
motto: There is no rose without a thorn.
clubs: Expert Machine Gunner, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: To his many collegiate friends he is affectionately known as "Rang". His ready smile and free hearted ways have won for him a host of friends who will always cherish the memories of him. One of his greatest characteristics is his ability to find a silver lining behind each dark cloud. "Rang" is never downhearted. When fate gives him a bad deal, he responds with a smiling face and a grim determination to win. "Rang", desiring to broaden his mind in the great field of science, decided to join the select group taking the Academic Course. This group thinks well of him; for, by his earnest work and determination to succeed, he has shown himself to be very capable in this field of work. We predict for him a great success in anything he undertakes to accomplish.

name: Rosamond, James Edgar
motto: I am not a politician and my other habits are good.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Plattsburg Barracks, N. Y.
description: In the fall of 1923 Rosamond was initiated a Tiger. He has worked four years, never giving up what he started to do. His work is always neat and accurate. "Rosebud's" ability to mix with the fellows has won him many friends who will always remember him. His word is respected by the professors as well as the boys. He has made his rank as a scholar at Clemson. Here's wishing you luck. May you always find happiness in your profession and future life.

name: Ross, Dwight Harrington
motto: I freely tell you all the wealth he had Ran in his veins; he was a gentleman.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
Secretary and Treasurer, Florence County Club '26
Delegate, S. C. College Press Association '25, '26
General Manager, Clemson Activities, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Reserve and dignity characterize every step as he rises upward. Gifted with a genial and magnetic personality, he is constantly winning friends. Never cynical or pessimistic, never downhearted, he is always willing to lend a helping hand to his fellowman. His personality and brainy head have placed him at the top in his classroom and in positions of responsibility and capability. His financial wizardry was manifested in his handling of the business of "The Tiger". Possessed of inherent grace and ease of manner, he is a Caesar among men and a Romeo among ladies. He is at ease whether it be in the office or on the ballroom floor. The future holds much for this democratic gentleman and the Class of '27 wishes him the best of luck. May he be the same friendly person, "Now and Forever".

name: Russell, Hamilton Earle
motto: The gentle mind by gentle deeds is known; for a man by nothing is so well betrayed as by his manners.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Handsome is as handsome does"; and as this true gentleman glides across the dance floor, as he carries himself among his friends, and as he mingles among the most elite of society, he is handsome. At ease in all classes of society, he brings cheerfulness to every group. If ever a man were gifted with grace and dignity, so be he; for it is a genuine pleasure to watch his elegant poise on the dance floor, or observe his grace of carriage as he leads his Company in review at parade. Perhaps it is this, along with his qualities of gentility, that makes him a universal "hit" with the ladies. His captivating manner, sunny temperament, and enviable disposition are the source of his wonderful gift of making friends. His habitual politeness and polished ease have given him the "coup de grace" of the finished gentleman. He is ardent and ambitious, and one of the brainiest men of his class. Always calm and self-possessed, he is a typical Southern gentleman.

name: Clynch, Townsend Salley
motto: Never trouble trouble, till trouble troubles you.
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Zack", for thus he is known to his many friends and associates at Clemson, has, by his manliness and fine sense of duty, won for himself a high place in the esteem of his fellow students. He is neither forward nor overbearing, but is always willing to take things as they come. Everyone is impressed by his irresistible good nature. Although "Zack" is not a star in athletics or a scholastic prodigy, he stands well in his classes and takes an active part in all student activities. We are counting on this lad to make a success in life for he is a diligent worker, and is a natural-born Civil Engineer.

name: Robbie, Oswald Sams
motto: Listen to every man's advice, but back your own judgment.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Robbie came to Tigertown in the fall of '23 from the "thriving metropolis" of Jonesville. After looking the situation over very thoroughly, he decided to cast his lot with the Electricals. Since that time he has been one of "Crip's" faithful proteges. Robbie has been very active in class athletics during his four years stay with us. His pleasing personality has gained for him many friends who wish him much success in his chosen field of work. The Class of '27 is expecting great things of you, Robbie.

name: Scott, John Thomas Jr.
motto: Worrying never made men great, so why should I worry?
description: This young man isn't a football star nor a "song bird", but he's a darn good fellow—genial, good-natured, and always with a smile for everyone. John is just a regular fellow. It is rumored that John's intentions are to further his education after finishing Clemson. The degree which he is after is not known definitely, but it is believed by his classmates that he is after an M.R.S. degree. John, we hope you find her. If you are lucky, bring her back next "Home-coming". Readers of this book, let me tell you with all earnestness, to know John is to know that which, in the code of all his friends and associates, exemplifies a gentleman. Always courteous, friendly and refined, he has established a reputation here as one who stands for those things which all the world admires. Luck to you, John, and may the best be yours.

name: Seaborn, Louis Augustus
motto: We can sorrow if need be tomorrow, but today is the time to live.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Oconee County Club
description: "Dit", as he is better known to us, claims as his "briar-patch" the thriving little city of Walhalla, and rightly so, because it is truly proud of him. Four long years ago this ever-smiling, cheerful lad decided to cast his lot with the "Tigers" in pursuit of more knowledge of Civil Engineering. Since then he has mastered his course with all its difficulties; we see nothing except success looming up in the future for him, should he continue to devote his efforts to his life's work as diligently as he has his college duties. Here we find a true and loyal "Tiger", one who has a smile, a kind word, and a friendly feeling for everyone. His good humor and genial smile have won for him a host of friends at Clemson. As a pal, you can count on him to stand by you to the last. "Dit" is indeed a worthy possessor of all honors bestowed upon him, because of his "live-wire" characteristics.

name: Sharp, George Washington
motto: Have a good time while you're alive. Death is so permanent.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: It was in the fall of '23 that boarded the train at Leslie headed for Tigertown. After looking the situation over thoroughly, he cast his lot with the "Butter Makers", as a disciple of "Big Ben". Since that time he has proved himself a capable student by shooting 'em all. He is said to be a "woman-hater", but we predict that one of the fairer sex will land him soon. We feel sure that with his jovial mood and good nature, the ability to make and to hold friends, great success will smile upon him, whatever pursuit he may follow in life. We, the Class of '27, wish you much luck in life.

name: Shore, Fred William
motto: There is a past which is gone forever, but there is a future which is still our own.
description: Fred, as this big-hearted lad is known, came to us in the fall of '23, from the little city of Greer, S. C. He realized the world's need for leadership in scientific Agriculture, and came with the determination to fit himself for the purpose of teaching this science. Through long hours of hard study and much burning of the midnight oil he has about reached his goal; and in a short time, will go out as a leader in his profession. Due to his cheerful attitude and good character, Fred has made many friends during his sojourn at Clemson. All of them proclaim him a regular fellow. Fred is a true "Tiger", and takes an interest in all college activities. He is the kind of boy that Clemson is proud to call her own. We are sure that you'll accomplish great things in life, Fred. All of the members of your class wish you a long, happy life, filled with success.

name: Smith, Clifford Twining
motto: Lose no time; be always employed in something useful; cut off all unnecessary actions!
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
Rifle Sharpshooter, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: To know Clifford is to know a gentleman of the highest order. Never pushing himself forward, but always courteous and friendly, he has won a warm spot in the hearts of all his classmates. Although he is an earnest student he always finds time for social activities, and it is needless to say that he is gifted with all the necessary graces. At all times his sense of duty and good judgment stand out and these qualities have made for him a high place in the esteem of all who know him. Unselfishness, the attribute of only a few, has dominated his every action. With so many sterling qualities, and with the best wishes of the entire student body, this lad is well prepared to tread whatever path fate may prepare for him.

name: Smith, Samuel Thomas
motto: Knowledge is Power.
description: He is one of Prof. "Cotton Future" Crandall's disciples and a good one. He couples his ability and head vision to advantage. Great things are in store for him despite the worries of being a father and husband. He has taken part in college activities, especially literary society work. When it is announced that Prof. Smith is to speak, everyone knows that something worth-while is coming. "S. T." is a gentleman, and a true, loyal friend. Not knowing him is a loss to anyone. Here's to you, my lad. We are certain that success will be yours.

name: Talley, Elias Smith
motto: Opportunity may knock but once; he is a wise man who is prepared to meet him.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Talley, we do not know what your intentions are, but we do know that something big is in store for you. We know this because we know what you stood for here in school—the clean, honest things that count most in life. Always remember, Talley, that the Class of '27 is with you at all times.

name: William, Gladden Smoke
motto: As you make your bed so you must lie.
clubs: Championship Baseball Team, Swimming Team, Camp McClellan, Ala.
Expert Machine Gunner, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: O Success, who is this but now added to your chosen few? What, no sparkling genius flaunting proudly his achievements? Indeed, it is but our old friend, "Bub", or William Gladden Smoke, St. Matthews, we pray that long may tranquil peace and progress be with you to aid in the harboring of such contributions as you have made to Clemson in the past. No sudden flare of ostentatious deeds or boastful words characterize this young man. Yet, lurking within the mysterious recesses of his ambitions lie the motives and the persistent determination that ever bring forth for popular approval the fruits of a faithful worker. Time only is needed to unveil the true worth of such industry. His quiet, easy disposition but reveals a beautiful mode of character attraction. "Bub's" bonds of friendship are truly bonds, and not to be severed by the frivolous, petty encumbrances of daily life. Maintain those ideals, Smoke, and there is no fear from us as to the degree of success that awaits you in years to come.

name: Spencer, Edward Pegues
motto: While I live women shall not rule.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Plattsburg Barracks
description: He came to us as a native South Carolinian, but he gets his mail at MacFarlane, N. C. In the fall of '23 he boarded the train at Cheraw bound for Mr. Clemson's College. He decided to cast his lot with the "Ags" and later to specialize in "Bull". He has made many friends for himself while with the Tigers. He has made many friends among students and faculty. While thinking of his friends we must not forget the members of the "Fairer Sex", for they are many. He is determined to know all that is possible for him to know about "Bull". Therefore, he has decided to seek more knowledge at Ames after leaving here. We wish you much luck in the livestock industry. May the best be yours.

name: Stephenson, John Andrew
motto: When the last great scorer comes to write against your name, He will not write whether you lost or won, But how you played the game.
clubs: President, Vice-President, and Chaplain, Palmetto Literary Society
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: He was among those who answered the call for loyal Tigers in the fall of '23 with a determination to master wisdom and understanding, he chose his work in the broad field of Agriculture with Agricultural Education as his major subject. During his stay with us, he has been an honest and faithful worker, and is worthy to be called a true Clemson Tiger. The people of Sharon should lift their hats to this lad because he has not only been successful in his scholastic work but his ideals are high and he is a man above par. He is a loyal and congenial friend, who always stands for the right. We have found in him qualities of true manhood: honesty, sincerity, and diligence. His winning personality has won for him a host of friends who are wishing him great success as he goes forth into the world.

name: Stogner, James Robert
motto: Though a man conquer a thousand men in battle, a greater conqueror still is he who conquers himself.
clubs: Rifle Marksman R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Robert to some, but left his home town of Hartsville in the fall of '23 to become a Tiger, and a better one cannot be found. Seeing the Textile Industry leaping to the front, decided to join in the great rush; therefore, he cast his lot with the Textile Engineers. His firmness along with his kindness has gained him a warm place in the heart of every "Linthead." We know there are big things before you, and knowing how capable you are of meeting these things, we are placing a "sure bet" on your success. May the best be yours at all times.

name: Stoutamire, Harry Lee
motto: Be true, for there are those who trust you; Be pure, for there are those who care; Be strong, for there is much to suffer; Be brave, for there is much to dare.
clubs: Clemson R. O. T. C. Championship Baseball Team, Camp McClellan
Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: The month of September 1923 was indeed successful in its undertaking of bringing to this institution a lad from Holly Hill known as Harry Lee Stoutamire. Harry Lee, as he is known by the boys, came here to join this Class of '27 with an excellent record from high school, and he most assuredly has not gone back on the record he made in the "Old Home Town". He is well known among the boys of the college because of the active part which he has taken in the various phases of college activities. It is with much regret that we have to part, Harry Lee, because all of us have learned to admire you. Your personality will win you many friends yes, especially among the fairer sex, no matter what your vocation may be. Keep up the good work, and always remember that the Class of '27 is your friend as a class or as individuals. We are expecting great things of you.

name: Sutherland, Edgar Stewart
motto: Any fool can go to bed, but it takes a man to get up.
description: "Dick," as he is known to us, thoroughly absorbed the Tiger Spirit from his brother and followed in his footsteps; thus reaching Clemson in the fall of '23. With a vision of the great things that remain undone in the Civil Engineering world, "Dick" joined the select group of those who are continually hunting lost moments through a transit. Now "Dick" is just an all-round good fellow, a true friend, and a loyal Tiger. Worries never seem to come this lad's way, yet he has won the respect of both fellow-students and professors by his conscientious work and good scholastic record. "Dick," we know that success will be yours. May the world proclaim, "Here's a man."

name: Talbert, James Hayes
motto: If you can't say a good word for your fellowman, do not speak at all.
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Here he is, the boy with the laughing eyes and merry wit. "Jim," as he is known by his many friends, journeyed from the chicken center of the state to Clemson in the fall of '23 to cast his lot with the "Aggies". After finishing his second year at Clemson, "Jim" decided that the farmers of the state should know more about agriculture; therefore, he joined the great host of "Crandalites" in order that he might be able to administer his knowledge to them. While at Clemson "Jim" has won the respect of the boys, the faculty, and last but not least, a certain girl. "Jim," we hate to part with you, but we believe that you will soon have better company and she'll inspire you to put your all into your life's work.

name: Tarrant, William Edward
motto: To live as gentle as I can; to be, no matter where, a man.
clubs: Sharpshooter Machine Gun, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Tyranny Ed", as we know him best, comes from one of the textile centers of the South—Piedmont. From the first "Ed" has been an ardent disciple of "Swine", "Pop", and "Brutal Joe", in fact not many men know as much about textile engineering as "Ed". The very fact that "Tripod" is so skillful in his profession does not mean that he has slighted the second phase of college life. The truth is that "Ed" has been an admirer of the fair sex ever since his High School days, but for this one fault, "Ed" would be the best soldier in Clemson's Army. To the I. O. R. M. much of "Ed's" success has been made possible for these brothers have stood behind him at all times in his climb for knowledge and fame. May you miss no chance to advance in life.

name: Thomas, John Richard
motto: Life isn't in holding a good hand, but in playing a bad hand well.
clubs: Rifle Sharpshooter, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Dick" came to "Tigertown" in the fall of '23 and cast his lot with the Electricians, but later finding this course too easy for him, changed to Academic. "Dick" is a well known figure on the gridiron, being one of the "main-stays" on Coach Goodale's team. He has been on the Scrub Team ever since his Sophomore year. While here at Clemson "Dick" has made many friends by his qualities which also make him a true Tiger. He is a man as well as a scholar, and he has those qualities that cause us to predict for him real success in life. Here's to you, "Dick". May the best in life be yours always.

name: Thomson, Daniel Park Jr.
motto: In the battle of life we cannot hire a substitute.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: "Dan", or perhaps better known as "Boone", hails from the town of Four Water Towers. "Boone" graduated from the Seneca High School, came directly to Clemson, and has stuck with us the four years. In his "rat" year he surprised the natives, as well as himself, by making the honor roll. He has kept up his good work throughout the various classes. "Boone's" ambition is to become a dyer. At the present time he is "Swine's" most promising understudy. "Dan" is a boy that is bound to make a success in life. He has one of the most pleasing personalities that is to be found in any boy. In his four years of college he has formed a character that has been unblemished. "Dan's" idea of life seems to be: "To complete that which I have started, in the most successful manner."

name: Turner, Charles Milton Jr.
motto: Nothing is so congenial as a clear conscience—Think for yourself.
clubs: Member of Winning Relay Team at Southern Relays
Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
Secretary and Treasurer Barnwell County Club
description: Charles is the fortunate possessor of numerous cognomens. He is gifted with the wonderful art of making friends. His humorous nature and friendly manner have made him an outstanding figure among his classmates. He cheerfully accepts his many nicknames. The Tiger football team was extremely fortunate in securing Charles as their manager. His marked business ability and inexhaustible energy enabled him to do much for his men. Besides his managerial duties, "Racehorse" has also made a brilliant record on the cinder paths of the South. His keen heels have spelt disaster for many of the best sprint men in the conference. Here's to you, Charles, may your sterling personality, clean sportsmanship, and amiable disposition win for you great success.

name: Richard, Theodore Tyler
motto: "Let him not belong to another man who may be his own."
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Although undecided for quite a while, Tyler finally decided to follow the line of great resistance in the Agricultural Courses—Chemistry. He came here to learn more of the methods of agriculture in order that he might utilize the great natural resources of Orangeburg lands. Tyler does a great deal, and talks little. Steadily "Rinkie" has worked with his studies and classes. The reward is apparent when one looks over the scholastic records. He has devoted most of his four years at Clemson to his course, and holds his honors there. When there's a task to be done, "Rinkie" is present to do his part successfully. Duty never calls that he is not ready and willing at any hour. What more can be said of a fellow when you can say that he has succeeded in his undertakings, and has proved himself a true friend to all who know him? "Rinkie", may your accomplishments not end with your college career. Your many friends believe in you and expect great things of you in later years.

name: Jaqueline, Gray Valentine
motto: "'Tis better to rule in hell than serve in Heaven."
clubs: Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: To know "Jack" is to like him, and who doesn't like him? He is a true friend, a congenial companion, and above all, a "good fellow." Always smiling, this blonde headed youth is ready for anything that arouses fun. He can always be depended upon to do his share of any work that has to be done. "Jack" enlisted, along with many others, in search for the elusive electron, but later changed his mind and decided to major in the Academic Course. This course will fit "Jack" for his work in later life, but course or no course, "Jack" is sure to succeed for he has all the inherent qualities that make a leader. "Jack" has taken a prominent part in athletics, especially football. Injuries at critical times have prevented him from making his letter. "Jack", you have the best wishes of your many friends for future success.

name: Warren, Joseph Albert, Jr.
motto: Think before you speak; then speak what you think.
description: What is better to be said of a man than to say he has a "hard to equal character"? Truly this can be said of "Joe". Yes, he is a song bird, too. Clemson has finished many a worthy Tiger, but in "Joe" she added to her history of men. One who has been a tireless worker, a faithful Tiger, a never-to-be-forgotten friend to every man. "Joe" is the kind of friend that men want, good-natured, courteous, refined and an all round prince of a fellow. He is originally from Sumter, but now he resides near the old ice plant on the Hutchinson River. The people of Mt. Vernon have learned to love "Joe" as we have. He is bound to be a successful engineer, for any man who has the intelligence that "Joe" is blessed with combined with his rare personality, and ambition to become great, cannot be kept down. "Joe", the old friends of '27 give their best to you, plus blessings of a Charlotte observer. "Joe", we are interested in you. Fulfill your ambition.

name: Watson, Lewis Pou
motto: Determination makes dreams come true.
description: After pursuing for two years at Wofford the path which leads to an A. B. degree, Watson decided that he had rather look into a transit than into dusty literary books. Therefore, he journeyed to the University of N. C. to study Civil Engineering. However, after being there three months, Lewis concluded that he should become a tiller of the soil. Thus, at the beginning of the second semester of last session, Watson became a Tiger.

name: Weigle, Charles Cummings
motto: To thine own self be true, and it must follow as the night the day, Thou canst not then be false to any man.
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Alabama
description: Well fellows, here he is! He hails from Belton, the thriving metropolis of "somewhere in Carolina". He came to old Clemson's Halls in the autumn of '23 in pursuit of the elusive Wisp O' Wisdom. As soon as Charlie arrived in Tigertown his dynamic personality began to manifest itself. His smashing disposition has reflected itself in the hearts of all who have known him. A sly human altogether likable, and a wit with a barb but no sting are characteristic of him. He is the type of man that must be called a friend—and what a friend! So Charlie, here's to you! We, the Class of '27, wish you the best that life can offer, and trust our hopes shall not have been in vain. We know that success will crown you. Keep on "Keeping on" old boy, the world needs you for it's on such as you that civilization depends—a man.

name: West, Casper Perrin
motto: Talk to me not of a name great in story, For the days of our youth are the days of our glory.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan
description: This handsome, blue-eyed, typical cavalier joined the Tiger Clan in 1924, having gone to The Citadel the previous year. He has those outstanding physical qualities that so mark the real man that he is. His whole person is a pleasing combination of good humor, stern manhood, and rock-bottom character. Casper is perfectly at ease in any company—probably a bit happier, however, in the gay hilarity of good fellows. He is the kind of sport that is most desired—in for a good time, yet sensible enough to know how far to go with fun. He is a true gentleman and a good soldier.

name: Walter, West
motto: Worry never made great men; why should I worry? Just roll on, Old World, and I'll roll with you.
description: "Playboy", as he is generally known, has become an established personage at Clemson. He arrived here in the fall of '21 and was met by a welcoming committee. Everyone seemed to have a good time but "Playboy". His ability at getting his name on the daily delinquencies twice earned him leaves of absence. He attended the University of "Hard Knocks" the last time, and since coming back to Clemson he has decided to settle down and finish. "Walt" has selected Civil Engineering as the means of mounting to fame and fortune, and his ability is sure to make this step a short one. All of "Playboy" is not play, because he can buckle down when he has to; it is this tendency that will achieve success for him in later life. Here's to you, "Playboy"; your host of friends in the corps of cadets wish you the best of luck.

name: Whetsell, Joel Arlee
motto: Or leave a kiss but in the cup, and I'll not look for wine.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: The thriving little town of Parler has one son at Clemson of whom she is very proud, and justly so. "Buck" entered Clemson in the fall of '22, and after his second year here, he decided to take a leave of absence for one year. Returning again in the fall of '25, "Buck" has gained quite a reputation among his fellow-students, and professors as well. Therefore, we can see nothing but success in store for him. "Buck" is always ready to act the part of a friend, or to lend a helping hand to anyone in need. A ready smile and a likeable disposition are directly responsible for his large number of personal friends. He has all the traits of character that go to make up a gentleman. We wish you well, "Buck", and may success be yours.

name: White, Alvin Henry
motto: Trust everybody, but always cut the cards.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This is the boy who is honest, noble, and straightforward. To know him is to like him. His congenial manner and free heart mark him as a true friend wherever he goes. From the beginning, it was his purpose to make college everything it should be. With this in view he set to work diligently to achieve his purpose, which he has accomplished without a doubt. His efforts have been gradually rewarded, for not only has he obtained a good education, but has made for himself a host of friends whose esteem he will carry forever. Truly a man's college career has been successful when he has proved himself a scholar, a friend, and a gentleman. After carefully considering the different courses offered in the curriculum, he decided to take the Academic course. This was a wise decision, for he has certainly become efficient in the scientific field. We are sure that with the good disposition he possesses, he will be greeted by the greatest of success in anything he undertakes. Here's to you, every son of Clemson son wishes you the best of luck in life.

name: Whilden, Carleton Nettles
motto: To be trusted is a greater compliment than to be loved.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This young lad came into our midst from the Gamecock City of Sumter. Sumter's loss was our gain, and immediately upon his arrival in "Tigertown" this smiling lad set to work as only he is capable of doing. Taking everything as it came, rejoicing in good fortune, and never complaining of anything—all these have been the characteristics of him. Throughout his college career he has been an honest and faithful worker and is worthy to be called a true "Clemson Tiger". He is a leader among his classmates, and his opinion is respected by all on all occasions. The people of Sumter should lift their hats to this lad because he has not only been successful in his scholastic work, but his ideals are high, and he is a man above par. Clemson is loath to part with a man of his calibre, and wherever he goes he may rest assured of the good will of his classmates and of his Alma Mater.

name: Williamson, Joseph Walter
motto: Keep the golden mean between saying too much and too little.
clubs: Southern Regional Council Y. M. C. A. '25-'26
description: "Bill" came to Clemson with the Class of '26 determined to put the finishing touches on his education. For some reason he was away for one year, but resumed work with the Class of '27. He came to us from his native town of Hamer, S.C., and took his stand with the "Aggies", later to specialize in Dairying. "Bill" has taken much interest in the Y. M. C. A. His work will never be forgotten by his numerous friends at Clemson. Everyone knows him as a willing worker and a friend always. As for the military feature of his College life, "Bill" cared very little, and he spent his time in service to his fellow students. Luck to you, ole boy, and may your happiest days of the past be your saddest days of the future. But in case you become lonely and weary, just think of your pipe.

name: Wilson, James Ulla
motto: Smile, and the world will greet you with a smile.
description: Those who know "Wee Willie" wish for no better friend. He's a jolly good fellow and a real sport. To know him is to love him. He came to "Tigertown" on the recommendation of his older brother who finished here, just to give the place a tryout. He liked it, stuck, and made good. Now he is listed among "Crip's" Enlightened Electricals who will go forth from Clemson to set the world on fire. As soon as Wilson landed here he was given the name of "Wee Willie from Villy Ricky." It is the only name by which most of his friends will ever know him. "Wee Willie" won his fame on the Cross Country Team here and in Boxing at Camp in Alabama. He's not the best athlete in the world, but he's the real kind—the kind that is never out and doesn't know how to give up. With his grit and determination we are sure that he will make a success of anything he tackles in this life.

name: Yarborough, Hugh Wylie
motto: Genius is eternal Patience.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Oh! Here he is, gentleman, the not-wanted-to-be-praised Yarborough. After arriving at Tigertown, he set himself to work, striving earnestly to adhere to duty in the strict sense of the word. His scholastic work has been successful, and his personality has won him many friends who will cherish him in their memory. We predict great things for this "Pete," for some day he intends to become the honorable Professor Hugh Wylie Yarborough. Our best wishes are extended to our "Pete" with sincere hope that the coveted Ph.D. will not be hard to acquire.

name: Youngblood, James Emmett
motto: An honest man's the noblest work of God.
clubs: Secretary and Vice-President, Palmetto Literary Society '26
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Attention!" No such command is necessary to those who know our popular Elko entry; for though he wears a sword dangling at his side, his power and eminence in the corps depend not upon it for stability. On the contrary, it is his calm reserve, his dauntless courage, together with his generous and amiable disposition that have made him one of the most outstanding exponents of leadership in the Class of '27. A man of excellent human dough who has the rare merit of feeling that if ever let loose, his talent would not set the world on fire; one when having nothing to say, abstains from giving us worldly evidence of the fact by asking us the length of a piece of string; but a man to the manner born, careful of dress, affable in his manner, and conscientious in his endeavors. If the members of his class should be asked to define "man," every finger would point with pride to the modest but striking figure of Youngblood. That he will be a great success is unquestionable; that his classmates shall cherish him in his triumphs a certainty. God speed, "Scrap!"

name: Kinard, Frederick William
motto: Know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.
clubs: Sect. and Treas., Pres. Chemistry-Science Club
R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Kinard came to us in '23 for the purpose of studying Chemistry. We all know how unusually well he has succeeded in his course. He has made an enviable record as a chemistry student, and as a student of all sciences as well. Nowhere is there a more ardent lover and supporter of Science. Not everyone at Clemson knows Kinard, but those who are so fortunate as to know him, know that he is a rare friend and an all-round good fellow. Long hours in the library and laboratory have not caused him to forget a certain girl in the old home town. Certain it is that she will always help and inspire him to achieve great things. We prophesy great things for you, old boy. Go after them.

name: King, James NoFlet
motto: Be true to yourself.
description: Jimmie entered Clemson in 1921 as a candidate for a degree in Civil Engineering. After a successful freshman year he decided to equip himself with a background of practical experience. He returned to Clemson in 1923 after two successful years of extensive road work. Jimmie possesses the faculty of thinking and acting instantly in an emergency; indecision is totally unknown to him. He has an irresistible personality and a spirit of "never say die." These qualities go to make up the real leader that he is. He is a confirmed optimist and fatalist, as evidenced by his pet phrase: "why worry?" He doesn't have to worry because he makes things come out to suit himself. He is a high jumper of no mean ability and was a member of the varsity track team. As for girls—well just take a slant at this Spanish-looking gentleman-soldier.

name: Jones, Robert Caldwell
motto: Instead of pitying yourself, think of the many things for which you should be thankful.
clubs: R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala
description: Your hand is warmly grasped; your eyes are met squarely with a pair of sturdy brown eyes that seem to understand; you hear cordial, well spoken words, in a musical mellow voice; you feel the magnetic appeal of an unusual personality—and Caldwell adds the name of a new but permanent friend to his ever swelling roster. Few men have the strength of character and broadness of personality that Caldwell possesses. Seldom do we find, mental alertness, perfection of physique, and the lure of attractive manner molded gracefully into one individual. Were such the case, Caldwell would not stand out as he does, loved, respected, and honored by a host of friends.

name: Keenan, Harold Earle
motto: I do believe in work, but I'm not in favor of it.
description: In this young man who calls Graniteville his home, we have a typical college man. He is gay, carefree, happy, and pleasure-loving. Yet he has not forgotten his college duties, and he now stands ready to leave Clemson with what he came after—a sheepskin. Harold first yearned to be a great soldier but after a brief stay at one of Uncle Sam's training camps, he decided the life was too strenuous and departed for Clemson to study Architecture. His marked ability and faithful work have caused him to succeed admirably in his chosen course, and he can now readily tell you the difference between the Corinthian Order and a municipal building. Harold is a "sheik", if the more gentle sex informs us correctly, and for this reason the sisters love him and the stags envy him. However, his popularity with the girls is eclipsed by that which he has among his fellow students. His pleasing personality, together with his ability to mix well, has gained for him a host of friends.

name: Dupre, Gilbert Clark
motto: "There isn't time in life to make two reputations so watch your first one."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Square and Compass Club '26
description: To Know Gilbert is to realize that a truer friend could not be desired, and that if one were to desired he could not be found. For four long years his classmates have known him familiarity as 'Dupe' and his quiet but humorous ways have drawn to him many life-long buddies. While no world's champion in scholarship nor in athletics, he has his mark in both these phases of college activities. As business manager of 'The Chronicle' and as a wearer of the most coveted of all awards--the Block 'C' he has written his name in large letters in the legend of '27.

name: Earle, Julius Edward
motto: "It is good to sojourn in every place as if you meant to spend your life there."
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R. O. T. C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: If there is anyone in the Class of '27 who commands the respect, admiration, and love of his classmates and other friends, that one is "Ed". His frank and kind-hearted disposition, coupled with his ability to perform difficult tasks, has placed him high in the esteem of all who know him. After staying at Clemson a while, "Ed" decided that he would be a Civil Engineer. As a result he has been building "air castles" for the past three years. Just how many of them will stand we do not know, but from his knowledge of the course he ought to build some real strong ones. Another feature of this young man is his "sheiking qualities". He has them, and they are often demonstrated on the dance floor by his captivating smile, magnetic personality, and striking appearance. Here's to you, "Ed". May your success in college be your guide for still greater success in life. So, "Ed", when you get that little bungalow and everything, just remember that all the "Tigers" wish you well.
